Title: Hellfire in the Heartland, 1938
Short Description: Play the Iron Sentinel, Pulsar, Marksman, Sir Kee Master, The Vigilant, Memnoch, Kid Phantasm, and guest-starring Dr. Cirrus in a mission to prevent portals from opening in Indianapolis.
Long Description: After the heroes stopped a ceremony in Central Park from allowing an extra-dimensional entity named Ciranoust from coming to Earth, the heroes must follow a stolen artifact and locate the kidnapped Memnoch. Mysterious portals have opened in the Heartland and "devils" have appeared. Join the heroes as they seek to rescue Memnoch, close the portals, and restore order to the Heartland. Role-Playing and Combat will be stressed
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Mist Coast Mysteries I: Tenebrosity
Short Description: A great, expanding cloud hovers over the ocean waters just off the Mist Coast, disrupting the local fishing industry. A group of intrepid adventurers assembles to probe the ominous mists. Are you in?
Long Description: A mysterious cloud has appeared off the Mist Coast, hovering over the ocean waves: immobile, slowly expanding, and impenetrable to the eye. No wind moves it, and fishermen dare not approach. Several fishing boats have gone missing, and the fishing town of Darnby lives in fear. Local authorities call for the assistance of adventurers to suss out the mysterious cloud and dispel it. As you enter these mists, strange shapes loom before you. Enter the world of veiled foes and threats that remain unseen - until it’s too late! While this is a stand-alone adventure, it is also the first of three sequential and interconnected Mist Coast adventures planned for Gen Con 2026. If you like, you can sign up for multiple Mist Coast adventure events to get the feel of a mini campaign. We will be following the Original Dungeons and Dragons rules published in 1974. Join us for an old-school D&D adventure!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Major League Dungeon: Indy Nationals
Short Description: In the heart of Middle America, dungeon delving has gone pro and you’ve just been drafted. In this high-energy, competitive D&D event, players take on the role of professional adventurers.
Long Description:
In the Major League Dungeon expect interviews, fan interaction, sponsorship opportunities, and a dungeon built for spectacle as much as survival.
 
 This adventure features four teams of 4–6 players, each at their own table, running the same dungeon under the same rules. Teams earn points through combat performance, puzzle-solving, style, crowd engagement, and creative problem-solving. When the dust settles, scores are tallied and the top team claims bragging rights as the best delvers in the league.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Mist Coast Mysteries II: Thylassophobia
Short Description: In an ongoing quest to rid the Mist Coast of a sinister fog, the party enters an otherworldly environment of cavernous spaces, deep and dark pools, and malevolent foes in this aquatic-theme adventure.
Long Description: After probing and dispelling a mysterious cloud off the Mist Coast and unlocking a cryptic portal, your party enters a bewildering subterranean world of meandering passages, huge chambers, fountains, pools, and portals. At first, the place seems empty, abandoned, and lifeless. But only at first… A mysterious benefactor seems willing to give you a helping hand. Is it freedom from this maze he seeks? Or perhaps something else? Join in the adventure to find out! While this is a stand-alone adventure, it is also the second of three sequential and interconnected Mist Coast adventures planned for Gen Con 2026. If you like, you can sign up for multiple Mist Coast adventure events to get the feel of a mini campaign. We will be following the Original Dungeons and Dragons rules published in 1974. Join us for an old-school D&D adventure!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: Phineas Bartleby: Court Date
Short Description: After effecting his capture and neutralizing his operation, your group is called as witnesses in the trial of notorious gangster Phineas Bartleby. Courthouse. High noon. What could possibly go wrong?
Long Description: This adventure continues the story of a series of related adventures from past Gen Con Conventions. Your group of railroaders first encountered Phineas Bartleby's gang as you tried to put a tunnel through a mountain where the bandits were illegally mining a rich gold vein. You then disrupted his gold smelting operation, stopped his train hijacking, and tracked him down to his mountaintop hideout, where he was wounded and taken into custody. Now, after a few months of recovery, you are summoned as witnesses at Phineas' trial at the Cedar Bluff courthouse. Riding the trail late in the day, you reach the top of a ridge and see the town of Cedar Bluff in the valley below. The trial starts tomorrow. In the gathering darkness, you wonder: Is all as it seems? We will be following the original Boot Hill Basic Rules published in 1975 for this event. Join us for a classic wild west adventure!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Mist Coast Mysteries III: Cimmerian Sentinel
Short Description: The seemingly indomitable Cimmerian Sentinel is poised to unleash itself on the Mist Coast. Can you solve the riddle of defeating the Cimmerian Sentinel before it escapes this dungeon?
Long Description: After freeing yourselves from an aquatic warren, the party discovers the apparently indefatigable Cimmerian Sentinel. In a race against time, your party must scramble to find a way to destroy this horror before it is unleashed on the Mist Coast. Clues strewn about a bizarre laboratory are about all you have to work with. But beware of the laboratory’s hazards, be they natural or preternatural, alive or undead, thaumaturgical or mundane. While this is a stand-alone adventure, it is also the final of three sequential and interconnected Mist Coast adventures planned for Gen Con 2026. If you like, you can sign up for multiple Mist Coast adventure events to get the feel of a mini campaign. We will be following the Original Dungeons and Dragons rules published in 1974. Join us for an old-school D&D adventure!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Intro to Miniature Painting
Short Description: Join Neil Duffey for an exciting intro to miniature painting. Learn the basics, gain new skills at any level, and take home your mini plus a PDF guide.
Long Description:
Join professional painter Neil Duffey for an exciting and approachable miniature painting class designed to build confidence and skill at the table. This introductory course covers essential fundamentals like brush control, basecoating, shading, highlighting, and color theory, giving you a strong foundation to grow from.
 
 Just bring your enthusiasm. Each student will receive a miniature to paint during class and take home, along with a helpful PDF outline so you can continue practicing after the session ends.
 
 While the class focuses on core techniques, painters of all skill levels will benefit from personalized guidance, demonstrations, and practical tips you can immediately apply to any project. Whether you’re brand new or looking to refine your process, this class is a fun, hands-on way to level up your miniature painting skills.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Intro to Glazing for Miniature Painting
Short Description: Join ImpendingDuff (Neil Duffey) for a hands-on intro to glazing in miniature painting. Learn smooth blends, shading, and depth.
Long Description:
Join ImpendingDuff (Neil Duffey) for a hands-on introduction to glazing techniques in miniature painting. In this class, you’ll learn how to thin and apply acrylic paints to create smooth color transitions, subtle shading, and rich depth on your miniatures.
 
 Each student will receive a miniature, a piece of plasticard to practice on, as well as a PDF outline to take home afterward. All paints will be provided, including a paint brush but participants are encouraged to bring a size 2 round synthetic brush for best results.
 
 Whether you’re new to glazing or looking to refine your technique, this class offers a friendly, step-by-step approach to one of the most versatile skills in miniature painting.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: Fundamentals of Light in Miniature Painting
Short Description: Join ImpendingDuff (Neil Duffey) to learn essential lighting in miniature painting. Understand light sources, contrast, and depth.
Long Description:
Join ImpendingDuff (Neil Duffey) for an introduction to the essential principles of light in miniature painting. Learn how to establish a light source, understand ambient lighting, and apply paint to create depth and storytelling through contrast.

 This class offers a clear, approachable foundation for painters looking to improve realism and atmosphere in their work.
 
 (This class can be used as a prerequisite for my Object Source Lighting class on Saturday at 6pm)

Title: Johnny Hates Jazz
Short Description: A new developer has bought Six Flags Jazzland in New Orleans, abandoned after Hurricane Katrina back in 2005. Accidents are plaguing the demolition that some blame on the ghosts of those who drowned.
Long Description: A new developer, Bayou Phoenix, has bought the remains of Six Flags Jazzland in New Orleans and have begun tearing down some of the 65 structures. Unfortunately, strange accidents have been plaguing the demolition, from simple equipment failure to catastrophic building collapses. Many blame the ghosts of those that drowned when Hurricane Katrina struck back in 2005. Others believe that the land itself is cursed. McCall International is sending your team in to find out what is happening before these hazards become fatalities.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Airbrush is Not Scary - An Intro to Your First Airbrush
Short Description: Build confidence with airbrushes! Join ImpendingDuff (Neil Duffey) to learn the airbrush, troubleshooting clogs, and common issues using a Badger 105.
Long Description:
This class is designed to build confidence and understanding in airbrushing for miniature painting. Using a brand new Badger Patriot 105 airbrush during the session, students will learn how an airbrush works, how to properly break it down, clean it, and diagnose common problems such as clogging, sputtering, and airflow issues.
 
 Twitch’s ImpendingDuff (Neil Duffey) guides students step by step through the process, removing the intimidation that often keeps beginners from using an airbrush regularly. By the end of the class, students will understand their airbrush inside and out, gaining the skills and confidence needed to troubleshoot issues and comfortably incorporate airbrushing into their painting workflow.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Sam I Am
Short Description: The city of Kirtland, Ohio just had a horrible Halloween night, with 4 people being murdered in various parts of the town. There were some paranormal sightings years ago, but never any attacks.
Long Description: The city of Kirtland, Ohio just had one of the worst Halloween nights in its history. 4 people were savagely murdered, in different parts of the town, and by different methods. This place was the site of several paranormal sightings several years ago, but there were never any attacks, let alone homicides. Are the two incidents connected in some way, or has something new come into the area to spread death and mayhem, especially on a night known for its chaos. McCall International is sending your team in to try to solve these bizarre cases before anyone else dies.

Title: Painting Realistic Gems & Lenses for Miniatures
Short Description: Join Neil Duffey (ImpendingDuff) to learn how to paint realistic gems, lenses, and glass effects on miniatures. Master light, reflection, and contrast.
Long Description:
Join Neil Duffey, aka ImpendingDuff, for a miniature painting class focused on gems, lenses, and other reflective surfaces. Known for his signature gem and lens work, Duff teaches the core concepts behind creating convincing shine, depth, and realism using simple, repeatable techniques.
 
 Students will learn how light interacts with reflective surfaces, where to place highlights and shadows, and how to use contrast to sell the illusion of glass and gemstones. A good understanding of glazing—or having taken Duff’s glazing class, available during this convention—is recommended.
 
 Students receive a miniature to paint and take home, plus a PDF outline. Students are encouraged to bring a size 2 brush with a sharp point, though basic brushes are available for use.
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Paint Your Back Log Lickity Split
Short Description: Join Neil Duffey (ImpendingDuff) to learn fast, effective painting techniques. Duff shows how to build speed while keeping detail. Mini and PDF included—perfect for armies or backlog clearing.
Long Description:
Join Neil Duffey (ImpendingDuff) for a hands-on class focused on painting armies, hordes, or clearing your miniature backlog quickly and effectively. Duff will teach practical techniques that maximize speed while maintaining enough detail to make your miniatures look great on the table.
 
 Students will learn methods for batching paints, layering efficiently, and tackling large groups of figures without feeling overwhelmed. This class is perfect for painting D&D NPCs, troops for wargames, or any collection that seems daunting.
 
 All paints and tools are provided for use during class. Each student receives a miniature to keep, along with a PDF outline to continue practicing at home. With Duff’s guidance, you’ll build confidence, speed, and momentum in your painting workflow.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Kids Intro to Miniature Painting
Short Description: Join Neil Duffey (ImpendingDuff) for a fun intro to miniature painting! Kids will learn basic skills, ask questions, and paint their own mini to take home.
Long Description:
As a father of an 8-year-old who loves painting minis with Dad, Duff has learned how to create an environment that’s all about having fun while still giving guidance to help young painters grow.
 
 Basic techniques will be demonstrated, and then kids will have plenty of time to paint, explore, and ask Duff questions along the way. Each child will receive a miniature to paint during class and take home afterward.
 
 All tools, brushes, and paints are provided—students just need to show up ready to have fun and enjoy the creative process in a supportive, playful setting.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: OSL (Object Source Lighting) in Miniature Painting
Short Description: Join Neil Duffey (ImpendingDuff) as Duff teaches Object Source Lighting (OSL) techniques. Learn to make fire, plasma, and lightsabers glow. Mini provided to keep.
Long Description:
Join Neil Duffey (ImpendingDuff) for a hands-on class focused on Object Source Lighting (OSL). Duff will guide students through techniques and concepts to improve one of the most commonly struggled-with effects in miniature painting.
 
 Students will learn about saturation, contrast, and the relationship between the two to create convincing glowing effects—whether it’s fire that radiates warmth, plasma coils brighter than a thousand stars, or intimidating lightsabers. A miniature is provided to paint and take home.
 
 All paints and tools are provided for use during class. Students are encouraged to bring a size 2 round brush with a sharp point, though basic brushes will be available for those who need them. Prior experience with Duff’s Fundamentals of Light class, available during the convention, is recommended to get the most from this session.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Branding Yourself: Identity Design for Artists & Independent Creators
Short Description: Learn how to showcase your work and create a unique personal brand that will stand out and authentically connect your art with your audience.
Long Description: As con-goers aspire to exhibit their own art online and as exhibit halls become increasingly crowded with artist and creator booths, the competition for attention is fiercer than ever. In this talk, I'll share my insights with you as an indie artist and former creative director in advertising, diving into the essential principles of identity design and branding and how to apply them to help showcase your artwork. This panel is specifically designed for artists and independent creators eager to present their work online and at in-person events and who want to learn how to create a unique personal brand that stands out and authentically connects their art with their audience.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Rapture Protocol: Part 1
Short Description: The crew of a small starfreighter on a resupply run to the remote Jeremiah VI industrial colony, soon find themselves embroiled in a deadly conflict.
Long Description:
Return to the roots of the Alien universe in the latest cinematic scenario for the new Evolved Edition of the Alien RPG. You are a member of the crew of a decaying old Bison-class transport starship, the USCSS Fidanza, making a simple run to the mining facility of Jeremiah VI to deliver equipment and collect a refinery tower. However, upon arriving at the facility, things are not what you expected. 
 
 *This is a 4 part game of the cinematic scenario Rapture Protocol. Purchase of a ticket for part 1 gives you access to all 4 parts throughout the con. All parts run at the same time on each day of the con. Only purchase a ticket if you plan on playing in all 4 parts.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Hope's Last Day
Short Description: Join your fellow group of colonists as you live out the last day of the colony of Hadley's Hope. Can you survive and escape before the base is overrun?
Long Description:
You are colonists from Hadley's Hope, a mining and terraforming colony on the barren moon of LV-426. While out on a survey run your tractor has broken down and calls to back to the base have gone unanswered. With no alternative, you and your companions make their way back to the colony to see what the hell is going on....
 
 Join the Great Unclean One as he once again steps into the Alien universe, this time with the all new Evolved Edition of the RPG! Come check out the differences between the old and the new while experiencing the fate of Hadley's Hope before the events of Aliens.

Title: Witch Hunt
Short Description: A group of villagers must find and lynch the witches among them during the day, while the witches kill a target every night.
Long Description: Unlike similar games, dead players on both teams continue to influence the game. Additionally, each player has a unique character with a simple ability--including the witches. This means the abilities of the witch team varies wildly game-to-game.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: Complete Darkness, Can we even escape?
Short Description: Your crew regains consciousness, chained down and in complete darkness, after a hazily remembered heist. Where are you, how did you get here, how can you escape, & what is that scraping sound?
Long Description:
Welcome to a world dominated by anthropomorphic animals (Djura) in a fantasy/sci-fi setting. Inspired by many stories of animal people, their struggles, victories, and how they mirror our own, the game is designed to be a comprehensive role-playing experience that enables dramatic storytelling of all kinds. Players will take on the roles of a Djura, becoming part of the story involving fragments, powerful remnants of their lost civilization, with the power to change the very fabric of reality. 
 
 *At the event, you'll choose a pre-generated character based on their preferences between 32 different animals and 8 different careers.
 
 **Attendees will receive an official set of Djura Dice and a unique code for a Djura quick-play PDF guide, which includes a starter scenario and pre-generated character sheets.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Thomas Marshall, Patrick Jascot, Aaron Wilcoxon, Teagan Rose
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Prove Yourself Worthy: The Path of the Immortal
Short Description: Your crew faced impossible odds to arrive at the Path of the Immortal, a mythical place of divine treasure. To seize the greatest prize, you'll face a series of challenges to prove you're worthy!
Long Description:
Welcome to a world dominated by anthropomorphic animals (Djura) in a fantasy/sci-fi setting. Inspired by many stories of animal people, their struggles, victories, and how they mirror our own, the game is designed to be a comprehensive role-playing experience that enables dramatic storytelling of all kinds. Players will take on the roles of a Djura, becoming part of the story involving fragments, powerful remnants of their lost civilization, with the power to change the very fabric of reality. 
 *At the event, you'll choose a pre-generated character based on their preferences between 32 different animals and 8 different careers.
 **Attendees will receive an official set of Djura Dice and a unique code for a Djura quick-play PDF guide, which includes a starter scenario and pre-generated character sheets.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Thomas Marshall, Patrick Jascot, Aaron Wilcoxon, Teagan Rose
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Dungeons & Deviants: A Devious Date!
Short Description: Is the eighth anniversary time for a dungeon date?! Play through a rousing quest with dirty jokes & unique storytelling gameplay that you help create! Each game is different!
Long Description: Cosplay Deviants is back again with their eighth year of naughty adventures! Your GMs will guide you (and a special guest Deviant!) into the depths of Erotic Fantasy as your characters attempt to solve a sexy mystery that each player helps to craft! Players will answer questions and roleplay their way into their own unique character. It will be rude, gross, sexy, and hilarious so please come prepared with an open mind. This is a unique system designed just for this adventure and we're sure you're going to love it! This arousing adventure is Powered by the Apocalypse and should be completed in less than two hours.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Monstrous Mushrooms & Hyphal Heroes: An Introduction to Fungal Biology
Short Description: Elevate your TTRPG experience with our fungal biology seminar. Designed for GMs and players, explore the diversity and interactions of fungi to inspire monsters, PCs, and environments.
Long Description: Learn how to enhance your myconids, fungrils, and fungi-inspired environments like never before. Explore how mushrooms, molds, yeasts, and slime molds consume, grow, and reproduce. Fungi display incredible morphological diversity, produce mind-altering substances, and thrive in extreme conditions. Dive into their interactions with plants and animals. Our goal is to ignite your creativity in crafting biologically inspired monsters, NPCs, PCs, and environments.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Introducing the Deviant Tarot
Short Description: Join the models and designers as we explore the creation and lore behind the Cosplay Deviants tarot deck! Exclusive print and beginner spread included! Basic decks provided for the session.
Long Description: A brand-new offering from Cosplay Deviants – Introducing the Deviant Tarot! Cosplay Deviants is excited to bring their new tarot deck to Gen Con and we’ve brought along our Deviants to help you in exploring this unique deck. The session will include a guided walkthrough of the deck, a unique spread sheet, and plenty of time for free-play. During the second half of the session, you will be able to practice spreads while the Deviants chat with you about your results. An exclusive Cosplay Deviants print will be provided with each ticket and a coupon for a discounted rate towards the Cosplay Deviants Tarot at our booth. This event is safe for work but is rated T for Teen. All reading and spoken content will be cute, fun, and safe for work.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: Last Call for Endurance
Short Description: Hey folks, strange rumor cropping up. Town out past the Frontier: lot of undead, not a lot of violence. Head over there, find out what's happening, and get the undead back in their graves. Simple.
Long Description:
Beyond the Frontier, deep in the desert past the last reach of civilization, lies a town that should not be standing, Endurance. The townsfolk work, speak, live; just as they always have. Save for the fact that none of them are alive. No ravening hordes. No blood soaked streets. Just an uneasy calm held together by their rotting will.
 
 Faced with extinction, Endurance prayed for a Miracle. And a Miracle they received. But, as their unlife fades by the hour, the truth can no longer be ignored. A preacher proclaims their form divine and demands it be preserved. Others whisper that the dead are stealing time that was never meant to be theirs.
 
 As tension rises and certainty falls, it's up to you to determine what Justice looks like despite mercy and destruction wearing the same guise. Are some Miracles too dangerous to let endure? Or is life always worth living despite not being alive?
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Hot Gate
Short Description: 1958 - the US Navy formed Task Force 88 to conduct Operation Hot Gate, a mission officially considered to be scientific research. Beneath the facade however, lay secrets no one could fully comprehend.
Long Description: You’ve been unexpectedly recalled from your current assignment to a classified mission briefing at Naval Station Roosevelt Roads. Deep within the Navy's inner workings an enigmatic benefactor recognized your unique skills/experience and secured your spot on this top-secret task. The clandestine assignment code named Hot Game carries an official mission: observe & report scientific research in the expanse of Antarctica. However rumors of unexplained phenomena in the Antarctic Convergence, unexplored dangers below the ice, & unspoken tensions among the crew point to a mission far beyond the boundaries of scientific inquiry. What secrets lie hidden at the frozen edge of the world? What forces have drawn the Navy to this desolate frontier? As icy winds and shadowed waters of the Southern Ocean close around you, one thing becomes certain: Not all missions return with answers - or survivors.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Call of Cthurnubyl

Long Description: 22-APR-1986: You are an agent within the United States government. You have been recalled from your current assignment for a secretive meeting at Frederick Sherman Field (Naval Auxiliary Landing Field) in San Clemente, California. Because of your particular knowledge, skills set, and previous experiences in life someone higher up in the inner workings of government has taken an interest in you. They have managed to get you on a tour of the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant in Pripyat, Ukraine, U.S.S.R. in the name of the Interacademy Scientific Exchange. Your stated mission is to observe the construction of the new #5 reactor scheduled to come online soon. However, all is not as it seems in the Red Empire.

Title: Operation: Luna Rubra
Short Description: Officially, the Apollo Program ended in 1972. Unofficially, Apollo 19 was a classified lunar mission, launched amongst fears of Soviet activity. What waits on the Moon was never meant to be found.
Long Description: 1974 - You've been selected for a mission that does not exist. Under extraordinary secrecy the United States authorizes Apollo 19, a classified lunar mission hidden under Cold War bureaucracy and National Security concerns. Recalled from your duties, you're briefed by NASA officials who speak carefully, omit details, and make clear this mission will never be acknowledged. Apollo 19 exists to investigate anomalous telemetry & inconsistencies recovered from earlier missions. However, concerns persist the Soviet Union has conducted undisclosed lunar operations, forcing NASA & U.S. intelligence to confirm what, if anything, was left behind on the Moon. As you sail further from Earth, radio delays turn to silence, and it becomes clear your mission is not about discovery, but verification. What was found on Luna’s surface was buried for a reason. Some truths were never meant to return home.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Death Storm XLVII
Short Description: You've returned the Hand of Odin to the Gods! You deserve a rest. That's exactly what you're doing, when the Ghost of the Dwarven Priest, Derdrumin, appears. Melchime is raiding the fallen temple!
Long Description: You recently went beneath the evil city of Bilkarfin, and performed a ritual to return the artifact, Hands of Odin, to Odin himself. You had to get past Melchime, a Mind Flayer Mage, and his mechanics, to get to the burried Temple of Odin. You succeeded, and escaped, but Melchime learned of the Temple. You met, and he destroyed, Derdrumin. He was the ghost of a Dwarven priest, protecting the sunken temple. It's been 10 days, and Derdrumin has reformed. He appears to you... "Melchime is in the temple. He and his minions are raiding its treasures. The temple has them trapped for now, but I need you to stop them before they escape."
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)

Something has been kidnapping the good people of Canbridge, and Doffin has hired your crew to assist him in solving the mystery. Find out who or what is behind the kidnappings and help Doffin discover an entirely new monster! Characters provided or create your own. Free gift for every player!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Indianapolis by Night: In the Shadow of Ramses
Short Description: The Pharaoh is missing. The city is beset by enemies from the outside and something dark stalks the streets of Indianapolis by Night.
Long Description: The shepherds of the city have tasked you and a coterie of neonates to investigate a series of mysterious attacks that have left several victims drained of blood and left where humans have found them. Mystery, Investigation and intrigue awaits.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Fostering Accessible Gaming Communities
Short Description: Join as our panel discusses tabletop gaming opportunities in communities with diverse needs. We'll share our experiences and you'll leave with how to put accessibility at the forefront of your events!
Long Description: We know that a well-crafted adventure can be life changing. Both the need for inclusion at the table and the desire to provide it as a GM are key, but how do you approach this? How do you actually go about making games more inclusive for people with disabilities? Let us share our experiences facilitating across a variety of needs and age levels and leave knowing how to put accessibility at the forefront of your tabletop gaming events to foster inclusive communities, so you too can improve the lives of gamers in your area!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Chris Hopper, Stephanie Campbell, George Bergstrom, Sara Nurrenbern
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Painting 3D Printed Horns Small/Medium
Short Description: This class will provide 3d printed small/medium horns you will paint that can be adhered to the forehead with spirit gum.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Painting 3D Printed Horns Large
Short Description: You will get to paint a printed and prepped large pair of horns that can either be attached to a headband or strung to be worn.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Making Lace & Ribbon Choker Necklaces
Short Description: This is an instructional class on how to make lace chokers from a premade kit with hand-sewing.
Long Description: This is a workshop where each participant will get to finish a lace ribbon choker with a cameo charm with premade kits provided. The kits will include tools, safety scissors, 2 needles, pins, and pre-waxed thread. Supplies in the kit would include precut lace, ribbon, and a prepped cameo charm. You will be taught step-by-step how to put the chokers together by hand and learn certain stitches. This would be a great course for very beginners who want an easy 1st project.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Making Fingerless Lace Gloves
Short Description: In this class you will be using a premade kit to create fingerless gloves with provided instructions and patterns by hand.
Long Description: This workshop is suitable for all sewing levels, learn how to make fingerless gloves from stretch lace with hand sewing methods. Each set of instructions will also include a pattern so you can create more in the future. If you took the basic to sewing class you can take this workshop, or have some minimal experience sewing.
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Basics of Hand Sewing
Short Description: In this class, we will give you kits so you can practice and learn basic stitches as well as how to properly seam rip, sew on a button, pin, and cut fabric.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Clank! 10th Anniversary: The Dragon's Birthday Party
Short Description: Celebrate in fiery style with a special scenario, giveaways, and prizes. Ten years after its Gen Con 2016 debut, the deck-building adventure returns to where it all began.
Long Description:
Individual tables will hold six-player games. Special scenario rules will be taught.
 
 Experience with other Clank! games is helpful, but if you're new to the party we're happy to help.
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Bringing Your Player Character to Life for an Actual Play
Short Description: Learn how to increase character depth and engagement for audiences.
Long Description: Player characters in Actual Plays should be engaging and memorable experiences for the viewing audience. Learn how to add layers by deepening backstory with key life events and adding motivation, fear and connections to other characters and NPCs.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Alix Jackson, Kaitlyn Lorando, Queon Story, Deejay Castillo, Abbigail Cordeniz
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Pedal & Drink Your Way Through Downtown
Short Description: View all Indy has to offer with a 100-minute tour of our beautiful city. We'll pick you up on our custom made beer bike & pedal our way to a local drinking establishment & get photos along the way!
Long Description: Looking for a break from the con floor? Our 100-minute pedal bar tour is the perfect way to explore Indianapolis between games. Climb aboard our custom-built beer bike and cruise through downtown with your group, enjoy music and laughs along the way, and stop at a local drinking establishment for refreshments. This experience is ideal to unwind, celebrate a win, or kick off a night out. Expect great city views, plenty of photo opportunities, and a memorable group adventure just minutes from the Convention Center.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Kinusaiga-Inspired Patchwork Art - Seascape
Short Description: In this hands-on class, participants will learn how to create a 5" x 7" patchwork seascape out of fabric and foam board.
Long Description:
Based on the traditional Japanese art of kinusaiga, this modified version will see students carve the provided seascape design into a piece of foam board, then wrap the carved sections in small pieces of fabric to achieve a quilted effect. This class will involve sharp tools and detailed handwork, so some dexterity and knowledge of tool safety is recommended. 
 
 All materials will be provided, but participants are encouraged to bring: 
 -your preferred fabric/embroidery scissors 
 -your preferred hobby knife 
 -fabric scraps (any size) to use and/or share 
 -vision aids 
 
 Please feel free to email me if you have any questions!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Introduction to Gunpla - Panel Lining
Short Description: Enhance your Gunpla with panel lining! Learn how to add depth and detail to your model kits, making them pop with professional-quality finishes. Perfect for beginners—simple tools, big results!
Long Description: Take your Gunpla models to the next level with our Panel Lining class! Panel lining is an essential technique that adds depth, detail, and a professional finish to your Gundam kits by emphasizing the molded lines and features. In this class, you’ll learn how to choose the right tools and materials, such as panel line markers, washes, and fine brushes, while mastering the techniques to apply clean, sharp lines. Whether you prefer subtle highlights or bold contrasts, this workshop will help you refine your skills and achieve the perfect finish. Designed for all skill levels, it’s a beginner-friendly way to elevate your Gunpla building experience. No prior experience is necessary—just bring your creativity and enthusiasm! By the end, your model will have a new level of polish, and you’ll gain the confidence to customize future kits. Join us and give your builds that professional edge!

Title: Introduction to Gunpla - Scribing
Short Description: Unlock the art of Gunpla scribing! Learn to create custom panel lines and unique details on your Gundam kits. Transform basic models into masterpieces with precision tools and easy techniques.
Long Description: Transform your Gunpla builds with custom scribing! In this class, you’ll learn the art of creating unique panel lines and details, taking your Gundam kits from standard to stunning. Scribing is an essential technique that allows you to add depth, symmetry, and creative flair to your models, giving them a truly personalized look. We’ll cover the basics of choosing the right tools, such as scribers and templates, and teach you how to plan, etch, and refine custom designs with confidence. Whether you’re aiming for subtle enhancements or intricate details, this workshop is perfect for both beginners and experienced builders. Bring your creativity! By the end, you’ll have the skills to modify any Gunpla kit into a custom masterpiece that truly stands out. Join us and discover how scribing can take your models to the next level!

Title: Advanced Gunpla - Customizing

Long Description: Take your Gunpla skills to new heights with our customization class, focused on plastic card effects, panel lining, and scribing. These techniques let you bring unique detail and realism to your Gundam builds, setting your creations apart from the rest. Plastic Card Effects: Also known as “pla-plate,” plastic cards allow you to add intricate custom details to your models. In this part of the class, you’ll discover how to cut, shape, and adhere plastic card pieces to create additional armor plates, vents, or layered details. From simple panel extensions to entirely new design elements, these techniques will teach you how to transform standard kits into personalized masterpieces. The class opens up with a Speed Building event of your Entry Grade Kit which you will be using during the rest of the class to customize and enhance.

Title: 5 Minutes to Midnight - AI and the Tabletop Gaming Industry
Short Description: From science fiction to hotly debated technology, AI is here. Controversy has followed, paired with utopian visions. One thing lis clear: AI will change the tabletop gaming industry.
Long Description: For over 30 years I have watched and participated in evolutionary change in the tabletop gaming business but I have never seen anything with the potential to revolutionize the way games are created and published like AI. This panel will explore the ways AI may impact our business across many kinds of work - creative, operational, executive, etc.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Copyright, Games, Licenses and You
Short Description: Protect your work with a copyright. Control how others use your work with a license. Possible? How? Why? 25 years ago I helped create the OGL to address these issues. Publishing in a post-OGL world.
Long Description: The internet is full of people who will confidently tell you what you can and cannot do with a copyright, what you can and cannot protect with a copyright, how and why you should use a copyright license, and most of them are wrong. Copyright law is a very grey, ever-changing field shaped by judicial decisions more than the written law. Recent Supreme Court decisions shape that law in important ways that invalidate a lot of internet legal advice. Three years ago Hasbro blew up the safe harbor of the OGL, and an explosion of licensing activity followed. This panel will explore the law, recent decisions, theories about copyright and games, and strategies for licensing.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Bring Me Your Demigods!
Short Description: Players, bring me your level 20 crazy characters for this epic one-shot. What's the adventure? I’m not telling you, at this level, you should be able to handle a few surprises!
Long Description:
Haven't been able to get your favorite character to level 20? Are you a DM that has some crazy build you have never been able to play? Well, this is your game! Bring me your min/max builds to face off in this epic one shot for experienced players.
 
 Check out my website to give you an idea of what you are in for!
 
 Your character can be any published class, race, and background (Sorry no Homebrew or UA). Use the “standard array” for your Attribute Scores generation method. When you select equipment, use either method (Starting Equipment or Gold), You may also spend up to 5000 gold on other non-magical equipment. You may take up to 20 points in magical items using the following rules: 1 point Common, 3 points Uncommon, 5 points Rare, 7 Points Very Rare, and 10 Points legendary. Again published magic items only.

Title: Worlds Within: Creating Immersive Settings
Short Description: A practical discussion about worldbuilding covering how to craft histories, cultures, and landscapes, weave lore into play, design divinities with cultural impact, and keep stories coherent.
Long Description: A candid, hands-on conversation about building worlds that feel lived in and ready for play. We unpack how to craft histories, cultures, and landscapes that create story, and how to tie tone, stakes, and consequence to every choice characters make. You’ll learn clear methods for adapting existing worlds without breaking canon, and keeping long campaigns consistent without losing momentum. We show how to weave lore into scenes so it guides play instead of clogging it, and how geography, climate, and cosmology become engines for story. Expect practical prompts, repeatable frameworks, and honest lessons from mistakes so you leave with tools to build worlds that invite exploration.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: Served Cold
Short Description: A narrative focused horror tabletop RPG using the Dread system and a Jenga tower. Choose from list of one shot stories and shape a tense horror experience where choices matter more than winning.
Long Description:
This is a narrative driven horror tabletop RPG focused on atmosphere, tension, and collaborative storytelling. The goal is to create a compelling horror story together through difficult choices and rising suspense.
 
 We use the Dread system, where actions require pulling blocks from a shared Jenga tower. A collapse triggers serious story consequences and often the removal of a character from play.
 
 Players sit at tables of six and choose one self contained horror one shot from two or three available scenarios. Each story is an anthology style experience built around moral pressure, escalating risk, and horror themes.
 
 Not every story ends in death, but many do. Survival is uncertain and not the focus. What matters most is the story that unfolds, the choices made under pressure, and the shared horror experience created at the table by everyone.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Worlds Within: Nation Building Workshop
Short Description: In small groups you’ll create your own nation: define borders, government, culture, economy, territories, history, then add adventure hooks.You will leave with a kingdom and a take‑home guide.
Long Description: During this two‑hour workshop you will choose a region on a map and build a nation there. Using a provided take home guide, we begin by naming the land and setting borders that follow rivers, mountains, and roads. We define government and law, then add culture: peoples, festivals, art, faith, and everyday life. We sketch the economy by resources and trade partners and layer in how magic is practiced. We place a few important sites such as capitals, major ports, and sacred places. Then we write a short history that explains how the nation became what it is. We close with an adventure hook tied to those sites and the nation. You will leave with a short nation write‑up and a repeatable method for building more. No prior experience is required, and all materials are provided. You will work in small teams, or individually, and share notes at the end.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Materials Required Details: Writing Utensil, Imagination

Players will explore fractured timelines, uncover sinister plots, and perform high-stakes rituals to prevent the collapse of existence itself. Along the way, you’ll playtest bold new content—innovative subclasses, unique races, and mechanics designed to push the boundaries of 5e. Every choice matters, every failure echoes across eternity. Will you defy fate and save time itself, or watch reality dissolve into chaos?
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)

Long Description: Hayao Miyazaki's films frequently feature important animals, from Teto to Totoro and Jiji to Yakul. Newhall of Anime Archaeology will cover cultural and content differences between anime and Western animation, and will recommend a variety of anime series that can integrate with your kid's school work.

Title: Introduction to Miniature Painting
Short Description: Looking for a guide into the wonderful world of miniature painting? Look no further! This class is designed to inform you about not only what to do, but how to avoid pitfalls in painting.
Long Description:
Embark on a new adventure in miniature painting! Come learn from Kyle Dalton, the artist behind Pale King Hobby about the wide wortld of miniature painting. With more than 20 years of expeience behind the brush, Kyle will guide you on the do's and don'ts of painting to set you up for success.
 
 This course will contain the following:
 -A brief look at the culture and modern history of miniature painting
 -An examination of the tools required and how to best obtain them
 -A look at miniatures and the materials they are made of plus how best to approach each and what to avoid
 -Live Demo
 -Hands on working time with a provided miniature and personalized feedback 
 
 Students are recommended to bring a brush. 
 
 For any questions, please email me at:
 
 palekinghobby@gmail.com

Title: Stitched & Noted - A Beginner's Bookbinding Workshop
Short Description: Explore the art of bookbinding by hand-stitching your own notebook in this beginner-friendly workshop. Learn about materials and techniques to craft three "Field Note" style journals.
Long Description:
Step into the world of bookbinding and create a one-of-a-kind notebook. This beginner-friendly workshop invites you to immerse yourself in the timeless art of stitching together pages to craft a stylish and functional “Field Note" style journal.
 
 We'll discuss choosing and sourcing materials, from selecting the perfect paper and covers to exploring the versatility of various binding threads. As you learn the essential techniques of bookbinding, you'll develop an appreciation for the craftsmanship that goes into creating a handmade notebook.
 
 You'll have the opportunity to infuse your unique style into every aspect of your notebook. Choose from a variety of cover materials and let your imagination soar as you transform a humble collection of pages into a cherished, one-of-a-kind companion.
 No prior knowledge or skills are needed and all supplies are included!

Title: Bound by Hand - Coptic Stitched Journal Workshop
Short Description: Create your own handbound journal using the ancient Coptic stitch technique. Learn traditional bookbinding from cover creation to final assembly.
Long Description:
Step into the rich tradition of handmade books in this comprehensive workshop where you'll create your own Coptic-bound journal from start to finish. Perfect for artists, writers, crafters, and anyone interested in the art of bookbinding, this hands-on class will guide you through every step of creating a beautiful, functional book using techniques that date back to the 2nd century.
 In this class, you'll master the fundamental techniques of Coptic bookbinding, creating a journal that opens completely flat – perfect for writing, sketching, or mixed media art.
 Each step will be demonstrated clearly, with plenty of time for practice and personalized guidance. This workshop is beginner friendly and all materials are provided!

Unleash your inner florist with this captivating hands-on workshop. Whether you envision a lush bouquet, a whimsical brooch, or a statement headpiece, this workshop empowers you to bring your vision to life.
 
 Experiment with vibrant colors, intricate embellishments, and innovative techniques to imbue each petal with character and personality. You'll embark on a journey to create stunning fabric flowers that capture the beauty of nature's blooms.
 
 We will delve into techniques for shaping and sculpting petals and leaves from an array of fabrics. Explore the art of layering textures, manipulating materials, and adding intricate details to breathe life into your floral masterpieces.
 
 Leave with a collection of handcrafted fabric flowers and let your creativity blossom in this immersive and inspirational experience.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Modern Hand Embroidery - Kawaii Stitches Workshop
Short Description: Start your embroidery journey by learning essential stitches to create adorable kawaii motifs! This beginner-friendly workshop provides a solid foundation in hand embroidery.
Long Description:
Embark on a delightful embroidery adventure and learn to transform fabric into charming, kawaii-inspired masterpieces! This beginner-friendly workshop is designed to provide you with a solid foundation in the art of hand embroidery, enabling you to create the cutest characters from the very first stitch.
 
 We'll delve into the world of essential embroidery stitches, each one a building block for creating playful designs. You'll learn techniques that add personality to every stitch.
 
 Whether you envision happy little clouds, cheerful fruits with faces, or adorable animal friends, this workshop empowers you to bring your own cute characters to life through thread. Perfect for anyone who loves adding a touch of whimsy to their crafting!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Poke, Prod, & Pin: Intro to Needle Felting Workshop
Short Description: Discover the enchanting art of needle felting! Learn about essential tools and materials, and create your own charming brooch in this hands-on introductory workshop.
Long Description:
Embark on a journey into the world of needle felting! In this introductory workshop, you'll learn the fundamentals of this captivating craft and create your own adorable brooch. Our experienced instructors will guide you through the tools and materials needed to get started, including wool roving, felting needles, and foam pads. You'll discover techniques for shaping, poking, and prodding the wool to create intricate designs and textures. As you work on your brooch project, you'll gain hands-on experience and confidence in needle felting. Whether you're a beginner looking to explore a new hobby or a crafter seeking to expand your skills, this workshop offers a fun and rewarding experience. Join us and unleash your creativity through the magic of needle felting!
 Perfect for beginners, and you'll leave with the tools you need to keep crafting!

Plus each player will be taking home a swag package containing the following: your character sheets, 4 "Skinny Minis" brand miniatures, 10 custom luck tokens, and a custom satin bag to keep it all in. Do you dare fight Skeletor's forces for freedom?
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Why We Play Games: The Psychology of Pleasure
Short Description: We play board games for social connection, mental stimulation, relaxation, and the thrill of victory. But what is really going on in our brains that makes gaming such a pleasurable experience?
Long Description: Gaming feels fun for a reason — and there’s real science behind that joy. Our brains react in fascinating ways when we play, especially through dopamine-driven pathways that shape why certain games hook us, and others don’t. By exploring how various game mechanics, events, and situations impact the brain, game developers can craft richer, more rewarding experiences, while players can develop a deeper appreciation for the games they love. This interactive seminar, presented by a Professor of Psychiatry, breaks it all down with clear examples and friendly analogies, making the neuroscience of gaming engaging, accessible, and genuinely fun to explore.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Lights, Camera, Azathoth!
Short Description: Quiet on the set! The cast and crew of Xanthous: The Tormented King have gathered for a long day of filming. The production is going well, but tensions are brewing, as THINGS begin to happen.
Long Description:
Xanthous: The Tormented King has been a best selling sci-fi novel since it was written, and studios have been clamoring to do a movie almost as long. The author was always staunchly against it, but now that she has passed, her grandchild has no such qualms. The film is in production and everything is going well, even if the cast is high maintenance, the writers are annoyed with everyone, and everyone is annoyed with the executives that just won't stay out of the creative process. Everyone has settled in for a long day of filming, but no one has anticipated the grim pall settling over the set.
 
 A lovecraftian delight with heavy character focus and moderate puzzle solving.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Creating Interesting & Memorable Villains
Short Description: In essence, your Villain defines your entire campaign, so your Villain needs to be more than "I'm the bad guy!". In this workshop, you will learn to give them depth, personality, & conflict.
Long Description:
In Role-Playing Games, your Villain defines your entire campaign, so your Villain needs to be more than "I'm the bad guy!". In this workshop, you will learn to give them depth, personality, & conflict. To make a villain your players will talk about years later, you can't have a bad guy that is the bad guy, just because he is the bad guy.
 
 If your villains are devoid of personality, memorable plans, and defining quirks, the final encounters won't carry the emotional impact that makes them memorable.
 
 What we have found is that defining your villain really defines your whole campaign. The Who, What, Where, When, and most Why! You will start with a blank page and see how to create a villain, and with them, your entire campaign.
 
 Join us as we embark on our 8th year at Gen Con.
 
 PS: Great for authors & game designers as well!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Creating Interesting & Memorable NPCs
Short Description: An adventuring NPCs should be an important part of the story. They need to be more than a healbot or someone to carry stuff. You will learn to give them depth, personality, & make them a real person.
Long Description:
As a GM, an adventuring NPCs should be an important part of the story. They need to be more than a gopher, healbot, or someone to carry stuff. In this workshop, you will learn to give them depth, personality, & make them a real person. If your NPCs are devoid of personality, history, and defining quirks, the players will treat them as if they are nothing. Make them interesting and memorable. You will start with a blank page and see how to create a NPC, and get your players to care. This is designed to be about adventuring NPCs. Once that join the party at the beginning or early in your campaign, and go with the party till the end.
 
 Join us as we embark on our 8th year at Gen Con.
 
 PS: Great for authors & game designers as well!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Mystery at Hidden Valley Ranch
Short Description: This is a Wild West-themed D&D one-shot set in a strange cattle town. For up to 5 Level 5 Characters. Players that send character sheets ahead of time will get a custom mini.
Long Description:
Giddy-up cowpoke, this is a Wild West-themed D&D one-shot set in the cattle town of Mascow, which unfolds in three acts, full of exploration, combat, and gabdern NPC interactions. 
 
 The town is experiencing mysterious cattle disappearances! Cows are vanishing and later found completely drained of blood. The locals are in a panic, with some suspecting the clothier Buck A. Rue, especially since he's suddenly started making leather goods when he previously only used donkey hides. Renowned cattle rancher Salvador "Sal" Edward Dressing seeks the help of your party to solve the mystery and keep his cows safe! 
 
 Great for groups but can also sign up as an individual. If you are unfamiliar with Dumbest DnD, we focus on the non-serious side of DnD encounters and one-shots, so this will be a silly, but appropriate adventure.
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Materials Required Details: Dice (preferably, but will provided if not), a character sheet (one will be provided if you do not have one)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Broadway 5: Scions of Storytelling
Short Description: Again, you are sent into the worlds of song. You are asked to bring balance to a new set of stories that are breaking without reason. Can you fix the stories gone awry before they break your own?
Long Description: For the fifth time, you are sent into the worlds of song, sent to bring balance to the newer stories that have strayed from their path. Memories long-forgotten have resurfaced to challenge your friend Melodia and break the worlds she tries so hard to protect. Can you discover what even Melodia has forgotten, and bring balance back to the rocky worlds of story? No prior experience with characters is required. 5th Level characters provided. Roleplaying: encouraged. Puzzles: likely. Combat: inevitable.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Short Description: Step into the boots of His Majesty’s Musketeers and adventure forth to protect France’s assets before the dreaded British steal a newly discovered form of gunpowder. All for One and One for All!
Long Description: As the Musketeers for His Majesty, the King of France, it is your solemn duty to protect the crown and all of her interests. Will you embody the Musketeers on a journey to acquire a new type of gunpowder, before the British get it first? 4th Level characters provided. Roleplaying: encouraged. Puzzles: likely. Title: Stardate Armageddon
Short Description: Statdate 6818.4: We have been ordered to Devron II near the Neutral Zone. The Enterprise must assist two unlikely ambassadors to end the Colony’s Civil War.
Long Description:
Captain’s log: The Federation has ordered the Enterprise to Devron II. This is an old colony placed strategically near the Neutral Zone, but the colonists are embroiled in a Civil War. The inhabitants cling to outdated ideas of freedom and competing ideologies. Two ambassadors, that I know well, have been assigned. They are from Harry Mudd’s new “League of the Oppressed” group. Vanna of Ardana and Shahna of Triskelion will find it a difficult task to keep these two warring parties from destroying each other.
 
 Personal log: the Shahna and Vanna that I remember would be ill-equipped to resolve this conflict and I believe having learned the art of diplomacy from Harry Mudd… well I have my concerns.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Vanna of Ardana and Shahna of Triskelion will find it a difficult task to keep these two warring parties from destroying each other.
 
 Personal log: the Shahna and Vanna that I remember would be ill-equipped to resolve this conflict and I believe having learned the art of diplomacy from Harry Mudd… well I have my concerns.

Title: Starting Your Own Board Gaming Company, Legally Speaking
Short Description: Seminar on legal issues FATAL to small business owners: (1) IP infringement, (2) consumer protection laws, (3) securities & franchising laws, and (4) key contracts clauses to be aware of.
Long Description: As a nine year Texas attorney, I can attest: an ounce of prevention is worth a pound of cure. What may cost only a few thousand dollars (properly papering up an agreement) can instead cost TENS of THOUSANDS of dollars to enforce (full blown litigation). This seminar is planned as a survey of key legal areas for future small business owners by discussing: how to avoid accidently infringing on another's copyright and trademarks (very basic); co-owner issues to be aware of when having or not having a partnership agreement or a company agreement; how to avoid accidently violating both securities laws (raising funds) and consumer protection laws (deceptive advertising) when getting your business started and taking your products to market; and a survey of each of the very lopsided contracts clauses you will find--but likely can't negotiate out--of a form contract with a major company.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: What’s Your Game Plan?: Turn Your Lesson or Training into a Game
Short Description: Can you enhance your teaching lesson or training exercise with the mechanics of board games in only 20 minutes? For educators, training managers, content & game designers.
Long Description: What do the lesson “Finding Citations,” the game “Trivial Pursuit,” and the mechanic “Bluffing” have in common? Your team’s mission is to brainstorm a creative active-learning exercise or game in 20 minutes, by mixing elements from play and board games. Forget video games; think LOW-TECH index cards, roleplays, & puzzling questions. Whether you need to teach the rules of citation or compliance, a “game plan” can help! If you’re an educator, training manager, presenter, instructional or game designer, you’ll learn how to integrate play & simulations into your classroom or workspace. This full-featured workshop begins with an interactive presentation, then segues into whole room active game design, and is kept at an optimum size so we can explore your instructional problems in depth. Led by a professor and author of The Allure of Play from Columbia University. (Workshop changes each year.)
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Joe Bisz, Carolyn Stallard, Tyler Roehning, Katherine Neil
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Worlds Within: Building Worlds for Story & Play
Short Description: An interactive session on place, belief, and power that shows how to turn each pillar into scenes, stakes, and choices for novels, comics, and TTRPGs through live Q and A.
Long Description: This session works with the concepts of Place, Belief, and Power as levers for story and play, showing how they shape settings and character choices. Through guided prompts and live Q&A, we turn details into momentum: scenes in a novel, frames in comics, and decisions with agency at the gaming table. Bring a notebook for takeaways to leave with methods to help your worlds come alive!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Redesigning Board Games for Instructional Purposes
Short Description: We will play a very well-designed game (e.g. Pandemic), study its mechanics, then design an instructional variant of that game. For educators, training managers, and learning game designers.
Long Description: Are you a teacher, training manager, content designer or learning game designer? We’re going to PLAY a well-designed commercial board or card game (e.g. Pandemic, Innovation, Forbidden Island) for 45 minutes. Next, we will study reference cards I created that meticulously examine that game’s mechanics and how to modify the gameplay to teach any school or workplace instructional goal. Finally, you will pick such a goal, and together with other smart team members, brainstorm a new learning game inspired by the game you just played. You will walk away with several ideas for enhancing your own instruction, and with some custom game cards and handouts! Led by a professor and based on his new book The Allure of Play. (Workshop changes each year.) If you'd like to see pictures and info about this 3-hour intellectually and socially bonding extravaganza from previous years, visit our website.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Joe Bisz, Carolyn Stallard, Tyler Roehning, Katherine Neil

 This is a Narrative system that is built on a foundation of Traits and descriptors to help you immerse yourself easily into game play. Recommended for newcomers and veteran players alike.

Title: Welcome to the Writer's Room: Creating & Publishing TTRPGs
Short Description: A candid conversation about designing, writing, and publishing a TTRPG. Each guest is a professional in the TTRPG creation space, including writers, designers, and publishers.
Long Description: Tavern Keeper Vixie Belle sits down with writers, designers, and publishers from across the tabletop roleplaying game industry to talk shop and share stories from behind the screen. This panel explores the creative, practical, and often surprising realities of making TTRPGs, from inspiration and design challenges to publishing decisions and marketing in a crowded space. Guests discuss what worked, what didn’t, and what they wish they’d known when they started, offering insight for aspiring creators. Whether you’re interested in worldbuilding, mechanics, crowdfunding, or getting your game into players’ hands, this conversation pulls back the curtain what it takes to bring TTRPGs to life.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Long Description: Using basic, low entry tools to paint miniature acrylic paintings on tiny canvases using the grid method, which is an easy way to learn how to draw AND paint. Reference images of simple landscapes will be supplied so everyone learns together.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Painting Tiny Fantasy Creatures the Easy Way
Short Description: Paint your own 3x3" acrylic creature on canvas using the grid method.
Long Description: Using basic, low entry tools to paint miniature acrylic paintings on tiny canvases using the grid method, which is an easy way to learn how to draw AND paint. Reference images of fantasy creatures will be supplied so everyone learns together.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Budget Stamp Making for RPG Maps
Short Description: Utilizing erasers and a lino cutter, you will learn to make stamps for fantasy maps, dungeons, or just learn the basics.
Long Description: Learn how to make printing blocks out of pink erasers to be used for RPG dungeons, maps, session props, and more. Learn the basics of printmaking!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Doodling Using Scratchboards
Short Description: This class teaches you to create art by scratching away a black ink layer to reveal a colors surface beneath.
Long Description: Scratch boards are a unique medium in which paper covered black ink is scraped off, revealing contrasting color(s) beneath. Learn how to doodle and make unique one of a kind artworks.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Coronation at the Court of Stars: VIP table with Amy Vorpahl
Short Description: A diplomatic visit to the fey Court of Stars on the eve of a coronation reveals layers of rumor, ritual, and political tension—especially when it becomes clear not everyone wants the truth known.
Long Description:
DMed by TTRPG veteran Amy Vorpahl and played on a dazzling 2×3 ft miniature set by NPC Charles.
 The Feywild runs on contracts, symbolism, and loopholes, and this court is no exception. Whispers of scandalous bloodlines and archaic fey contracts puncture every conversation, as this coronation might have more at stake than meets the eye. Will the stars align to see this coronation through before time runs out?
 This adventure blends social intrigue, mystery, and cinematic action, culminating in a very public reckoning where the outcome—and even the crown itself—rests in the players’ hands.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Bringing Fire's Practical GMing
Short Description: A fast-moving, fun, hands-on exercise for novice, aspiring, and skill-seeking Game Masters where you work together to design a dungeon adventure and take turns running it with expert feedback.
Long Description: Do you plan on running a game for friends or want to be a Game Master at a convention? If you’re interested in honing your craft as a GM for any reason, this two-hour hands-on workshop is for you. You’ll collaborate with others to practice a “dungeon storming” technique that develops adventure design skills. Then you’ll learn about the “core loop” of GMing before you take turns in the GM’s chair to practice, with guidance and advice from a participant-coach who will play at your table. It gives you a chance to develop your flexibility, adaptiveness, and improvisational ability in an entertaining and enjoyable way as we celebrate the art of the GM together.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Bringing Fire's Practical GMing In Depth
Short Description: A hands-on deep dive for novice, aspiring, and skill-seeking Game Masters to practice their adventure design and game-running, with expert advice and constructive feedback.
Long Description: This four-hour workshop gives you an intensive, hands-on experience to explore the theory and practice of GMing. We’ll focus on what it takes to get an adventure ready for the table, and then practice game-running as a skill involving what we call the “core GM loop” that gets everyone imagining in the same direction. A participant-coach will be at the table as you take your as GM for the adventure you’ve prepped together. We’ll discuss different play-styles and techniques for responding to what players offer in the moment. You’ll walk away with a stronger skill base and portfolio of tools to use in preparing and running dynamic and engaging play sessions. You’ll develop your flexibility, adaptiveness, and improvisational ability in an entertaining and enjoyable way as we celebrate the art of the GM together.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Learn to English Paper Piece: Small D20 or Rupee
Short Description: In this class we will take a look at the basics of English Paper Piecing, a form of hand quilting.
