Title: Intro to Minipainting
Short Description: Miniature painting is fun, but sometimes people can get discouraged by a personal roadblock. This course seeks to equip beginners with my method of dealing with those roadblocks while having fun!
Long Description: You will be given a miniature to keep as well as a large brush and paints to learn the following: How to properly load a brush to quickly apply basecoats to a figure, how to fix common mistakes, how to paint a base without basing materials, and how to apply additional highlights to make the miniature pop. This class is will go over all essential tools, paints, and techniques to get your hobby a rock-solid foundation.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Power Word Unity: Representing Culture in TTRPGS
Short Description: Join Vixie Belle and Carlos Salazar, collaborators on the Power Word Unity Project, along with friends of the table to discuss cultural representation in TTRPGs.
Long Description: A candid conversation on cultural representation and cultural sensitivity in TTRPGS. Carlos Salazar, founder of the Rol Latine Discord channel and Bipoc creator Vixie Belle sit down with other Bipoc creators and cultural consultants to talk about culture and ttrpgs, how to do it in a way that's both kind and mindful.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Short Description: Looking for a TTRPG that truly fits your style? Content creator RadioFreeWill will help you discover your perfect TTRPG match!
Long Description: Have you ever wondered if there’s a TTRPG out there that fits you almost perfectly? Content creator RadioFreeWill is hosting an exciting speed dating style game designed to help you find your perfect TTRPG match!

Title: Become the Chef: the Beginner's Guide to Miniature Paints
Short Description: Much like how good chefs know their ingredients to flavor their dish, a good minipainter knows their paints to flavor their model. Here we will learn what's in the paint, and how to use it!
Long Description: Knowing what you're painting with is just as important as knowing what you want to paint. In this course, each person will be given a cube on which we will test multiple types of paints such as: branded miniature paints, metallic paints, heavy body acrylics, airbrush paints, inks, and fluorescents. We will demonstrate how these different ingredients perform over many different surfaces, and then use this knowledge to practice a variety of painting techniques such as layering and wet blending to cover over our previous work. All tools and paints will be provided. However, feel free to bring anything you would like to learn with, but please do not eat the paint!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Savage Scavenger
Short Description: You and your teammates are on a scavenger hunt to find three objects to unlock a final prize before the other teams get to it. Use your wits, your weapons, whatever it takes, to be the first there!
Long Description: Players will be divided equally into three teams who are playing against each other in real time to reach the final prize. You will be building characters who, as a team, may have to solve puzzles, fight, or finesse their way to the prize. We'll help you build your character and then we'll dive right in. If you have a large party, be advised you may be split amongst the three teams. Once the scavenger hunt begins, pretty much anything goes! PVP is a possibility, but don't worry, your characters won't be able to die until the very end, so you'll be able to play the whole time. We encourage players of all skill levels to join us; this is a community where we're playing for fun, and we would love to share that fun with both those new to the game, and veterans to the RPG world. We look forward to seeing you at Gen Con. Happy gaming!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: William Thorndike, Elizabeth Krueger, Jordan Gwiazdowski, Alan Urbin
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Magnificent Seven Samurai
Short Description: An homage to classic films Seven Samurai (1954) and Magnificent Seven (1960), this challenging but accessible game sees samurai and cowboys work together to defend a village from bandits.
Long Description:
Using custom classes, weapons, and rules, players will form a ragtag group of heroes to defend Okutama village. The group will be hopelessly outnumbered, and will need to enlist the help of the villagers, carefully strategize, and use the terrain to full advantage if they want to succeed. 
 
 	This adventure is suitable for players of all experience levels. Prefabricated character sheets with the custom subclasses for samurai and gunslingers will be provide. The story offers plenty of opportunity for immersive roleplay and lends itself well to complicated characters with strong arcs, but, as in the films, expect a few graves to be filled by the time the end credits roll - the villagers of Okumura need heroes who aren’t afraid to lay down their lives for a good cause.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Sewing Kit Cookie Tin Mimic
Short Description: Participants will receive supplies including a cookie tin, fangs, eyes, felt and foam to design and build a sewing kit mimic.
Long Description: Is it a tin of cookies? Is it a sewing kit? Is it a dangerously hungry mimic? Unleash your crafting skills to create a dungeon dweller that can serve as a sewing supply holder too. Only basic crafting skills required for cutting and hot glue use.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Bringing Combat to Life
Short Description: The key to crafting thrilling combat encounters is understanding the root of what makes combat engaging in the first place. This panel covers how to make every encounter memorable.
Long Description: TTRPG combat done right is thrilling, but too often it ends up running too long and becoming a boring grind. To craft consistently excellent encounters, we need to understand what makes combat engaging in the first place. This panel will introduce you to the golden rule that all narrative encounters should follow, and show you how to apply it to every game. We’ll also cover five tried-and-true techniques to keep your combat encounters fresh, engaging, and fun!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Whisperstone - a puzzly adventure
Short Description: Adventures await in Wildenir! As animal kin, you must work together to protect the magical world. Join us for an introductory session to our new role-playing system for all ages & experience levels.
Long Description: No pencils and paper needed - only your imagination! We’ll get you roleplaying faster. Learn to play as you explore the Thornwood Forest - meeting some real characters, solving puzzles, and maybe fighting an enemy or two! You’ll embark on an important training mission that might just be critical to the survival of the forest and the world, where the loose alliance of the Woven Nations is protecting knowledge and nature against the dreaded Collectors. How will you work together to complete the mission? Sessions will be GM’d by our team.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Adventures in Middle Earth: The Eaves of Mirkwood
Short Description: Following the defeat of the Dragon, King Bard has sent out a clarion call to the resilient folk of Lake-town, inviting brave souls to help reclaim and rebuild the shattered North.
Long Description: Set on the treacherous northern fringes of the Great Forest, The Eaves of Mirkwood serves as a harrowing introduction to the perils of Middle-earth for a company of new heroes. Tasked with escorting a group of woodmen or seeking passage through the suffocating gloom, the adventurers soon find themselves ensnared in a web of ancient malice as they encounter the greedy forest-dwellers and the creeping shadow of the Necromancer’s influence. The journey culminates in a desperate struggle for survival against a formidable foe in the heart of the woods, forcing the party to balance their survival instincts against the noble call of the Free Peoples. It is a quintessential tale of "there and back again" that highlights the fragile boundary between the warmth of the campfire and the hungry dark of the Wild.

Title: There's Gold in Them Thar Hills!
Short Description: Gold has been discovered in the Black Hills. The Natives consider the mountains sacred. And the Spirits of Paha Sapa are not happy to be disturbed.
Long Description:
Driven by the chance to make a fortune, you have come to the Black Hills in the Dakota Territory. There had been rumors for many years, but Custer's Army Expedtion of 1874 has found gold! The Sioux and Cheyenne are not happy to have their sacred grounds invaded by thousands of white folk.
 The spirits of Paha Sapa are also unhappy to be disturbed. The natives have stories of strange creatures who roam the hills. Miners claim to hear strange cries in the night.
 Are you willing to risk more than your life for the chance to become one of the wealthy ones?
 An original Call of Cthulhu adventure using parts of the Down Darker Trails setting. Characters will be provided, rules will be taught.
 Trigger Warnings: Cosmic Horror, Body Horror, Historical Accuracy
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Echoes of the Other Side
Short Description: Champions of the Magistrate is a week-long festival arena event. Will the party help overthrow—or defend the city of Everstar? Expect high-stakes choices, exploration, and a mini-dungeon adventure.
Long Description:
Champions of the Magistrate is a week-long festival and arena spectacle that draws crowds, contenders, and political intrigue to the city of Everstar. While champions clash for public glory in the arena, the real battle unfolds beyond the cheers and banners.
 
 The party is tasked with exploring a long-forgotten ruined temple on the city’s outskirts, seeking a powerful magical artifact tied to the festival’s outcome. This relic could either secure victory for the Magistrate’s favored champion—or tip the balance against them, weakening the government’s grip on power.
 
 As the festival rages on above, the party delves into ancient halls, hidden dangers, and forgotten secrets, knowing their choices will ripple back to Everstar. What they recover—and who they choose to aid—may decide not only the champion’s fate, but the future of the city itself.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Terror on Gilligan's Island
Short Description: Screams drown out the laugh track when cosmic horrors descend upon Gilligan's Island. Can the castaways learn the terrible truth of the Island and stop Gilligan's zany plan Before It's Too Late(TM)?
Long Description: Return with us to those gullible days of 1964, when 3-day storms and uncharted desert islands appearing out of nowhere seemed totally normal! Life as a castaway isn't really too bad. You live on a tropical island with clean water and plenty of food. You and your companions have built shelters and settled into a comfortable, albeit boring, life. You miss home, but you're doing okay. But the idyllic tropical scene is shattered on the morning that Gilligan goes missing. Will the castaways find him? Will they learn the truth of why they are here, and uncover the dark, dark secret of Gilligan's Island? Quantum Black is a cinematic, story-driven RPG powered by the lightning fast rules of the Ubiquity Roleplaying System. Characters will be provided, rules will be taught. Bring dice, pencil and sense of fun! Trigger warnings: Cosmic horror, body horror.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Short Description: In Part Two, the party returns to Everstar as the festival reaches its climax. They may be drawn directly into the fighting before roaring crowds—or pulled into the shadows of the undercity.

The arena battles begin in earnest. With the magical item now in play, the balance of power shifts, and every faction moves to secure the outcome they desire.
 
 Depending on the party’s choices, they may be drawn directly into the arena—fighting before roaring crowds—or pulled into the shadows of the undercity, where deals, betrayals, and secret struggles unfold alongside the public spectacle.  By the festival’s end, the fate of the arena, the champion, and the city itself hangs in the balance. The party’s actions will determine who claims victory—and whether the seat of the Magistrates stands, falls, or changes hands entirely.

Title: GM 101: Introduction to Gamemastering
Short Description: Keeper, Dungeon Master, Game Master…there are many names for the job. Thinking about taking the reins at your game table? This seminar focuses on concerns common among new GMs.
Long Description: The primary goal of this seminar is to address the anxieties and concerns that are most common for new and novice gamemasters, but all are welcome regardless of experience level. We will talk about fears, improvisation, safety tools, "session zero", preparation, and many other topics. This is a game agnostic session, and will not focus on the mechanics of any particular system. It is about all the other parts of GMing, which might not be as obvious, but are really at the core of what it means to be a good GM. Bring your questions, bring a friend, but most importantly bring your imagination, an open mind, a sense of adventure, and a sense of humor.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: GM 201: The Art of Conversational GMing
Short Description: Do your RPG sessions feel stale? Are you trying to take things to a higher level, but feel like you're playing a board game? Come learn about conversational GMing and inject some life into your RPGs.
Long Description: A conversational approach is more natural than you might think, and draws in more creativity from players at the table. The more invested everyone is, the better the game. The system you're playing is irrelevant. You can make Conversational GMing a part of anything from D&D to FATE and anything in between. The event is open to all regardless of experience level. Players are welcome too!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Long Description: A conversational approach is more natural than you might think, and draws in more creativity from players at the table. The more invested everyone is, the better the game. The system you're playing is irrelevant. You can make Conversational GMing a part of anything from D&D to FATE and anything in between. The event is open to all regardless of experience level. Players are welcome too!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: DIY Gaming Terrain: From Trash to Tabletop
Short Description: Come build tabletop terrain for your TTRPG out of household debris and bits that most gamers have on hand.
Long Description: Learn to make tabletop terrain with things you probably have around the house or are easy to obtain. This class will teach people the fundamentals of creating great tabletop terrain at home. We will focus on basic items that you can collect from the hardware store and stuff that you're probably getting ready to throw away. Attendees need only bring their imagination and a love for their favorite tabletop game. We will supply materials or you can choose to build one of our kits to create a small to medium sized piece of terrain. Styles of terrain you can make vary from Medieval, Sci-Fi or Dystopian Future but ultimately It's up to you what you make. You will make a piece of terrain that you can take pride in for your tabletop gaming.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Eyes of Paldina
Short Description: Last time the Nomads attacked the Capitol, it burned for days. Rumors suggest another attack is imminent. Go forth to settle your debt to the Caliph and save the kingdom! May Fate smile upon you!
Long Description: The burning sand holds many secrets - many dangers. In the Ivory Walls of Nawakesh merchant's rumors and whispers of astrologers warn of a coming storm: a Nomad storm. The last time the Hamazi Nomads attacked the Capitol, it burned for three days. You owe the Caliph a heavy debt and your markers have been called in. Cross the River of Sand, stop the Hawazi, and save the Caliphate to erase your ledger. May Fate smile upon you!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Division K: The Roaring Adirondacks!
Short Description: Division K Rule 1: Do Not Engage - leave that to Quantum Black. We’ve discovered an abandoned facility. Finders keepers! You've been dispatched to the Adirondacks. Check it out, Be Safe. Don’t die!
Long Description: Division K's first rule is Do Not Engage. We leave that sort of thing to the Quantum Black Wolf Spiders. Your job is to assess, consider, and report. A QB Low-Orbit Reconnaissance Drone (LORD) has discovered a facility in the Adirondacks. After several days of recon, it seems abandoned. So, in the spirit of Finders Keepers, you are being dispatched to check the place out. It should be perfectly safe (we think). Good luck!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: A Grey and Lonely Place
Short Description: On the North side of Bastion sits a Colossus. No one remembers why it is there. But yesterday, it disappeared. Now tremors rock the city! Go forth, Delvers! Find what Arcanum is causing this!
Long Description: In the North of Bastion sits the Synoba Plaza. It features a curious attraction. The Synobian Colossus sat overlooking the site of the last interdimensional incursion. Most modern scholars don't remember what it did, who made it, or why it should be left alone. BUT, within the last 2 days, rumors have circulated that the Colossus is missing! These rumors have made their way to the August Body of the Delver's Collective! Tremors have rocked Bastion and have been felt as far away as South Bastion. You've been dispatched to see what jiggery-pokery has led to this. Discover why the Colossus has left its post and if there is a way to correct this. Also: there must be some Arcana behind this! We do love Arcana.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Group: University of North Texas Media Library
Title: You Should Certainly Try - Making Actual Play Performances for Library Outreach
Short Description: Wait, a library made an actual play D&D show? Yep. This behind-the-scenes talk explores why we did it, how we built it, and why you don’t need a studio to try it.
Long Description: A university library built an audience-facing actual play D&D show. On purpose. You Should Certainly Try is a behind-the-scenes look at how Live D&D at UNT and The Hidden Stacks were designed as intentional media projects, not games that happened to be filmed. This seminar focuses on the thinking behind the work: why actual play was the right format, why making it public mattered, and how creative vision, student involvement, and institutional realities were balanced from the start. Rather than teaching gameplay, this session explores the design choices that shaped the project, what made it sustainable, and why this kind of work can succeed outside of studios and major platforms. Attendees will leave with a clearer sense of what makes audience-facing actual play work, and the confidence to decide whether they should certainly try something similar in their own space.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Bringing Fire's Practical GMing In Depth
Short Description: A hands-on deep dive for novice, aspiring, and skill-seeking Game Masters to practice their adventure design and game-running, with expert advice and constructive feedback.
Long Description: This four-hour workshop gives you an intensive, hands-on experience to explore the theory and practice of GMing. We’ll focus on what it takes to get an adventure ready for the table, and then practice game-running as a skill involving what we call the “core GM loop” that gets everyone imagining in the same direction. A participant-coach will be at the table as you take your as GM for the adventure you’ve prepped together. We’ll discuss different play-styles and techniques for responding to what players offer in the moment. You’ll walk away with a stronger skill base and portfolio of tools to use in preparing and running dynamic and engaging play sessions. You’ll develop your flexibility, adaptiveness, and improvisational ability in an entertaining and enjoyable way as we celebrate the art of the GM together.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: A Balefull Enigma
Short Description: A monster-focused murder mystery for level 7 characters. Interrogate witnesses, consult your monster-hunter guide, and avenge death in a not-so-sleepy town.
Long Description: You are a member of the Gallop: seasoned monster-hunters that ordinary townsfolk would best keep away from. However, when missing sheep escalate into a torn apart villager, it becomes clear that those townsfolk are way out of their league. With a provided investigation journal brimming with notes on local 'fauna,' and an assortment of weapons and potions ready to slay them, saving this town is another grueling day on the job for you. You may bring a 7th level character made using the 2024 character rules, but pregens will also be provided. Dice and pencils provided if needed. Magic items will be provided during the game.

Title: GM & Write Like a Druid: Animals
Short Description: Animals are common in RPGs and spec fic. Make your world more immersive with real behavior, ecology, and history of human-animal interactions. Taught by a scientist versed in biology and education.
Long Description: Do you want your dungeon ecosystem to make more sense? Did using Speak with Animals in Baldur’s Gate 3 have you wanting to voice your own animal NPCs? Writing horror and want some inspiration from disturbing animal facts? Or maybe you’re a history fan who wants the use of animals in your setting to be more authentic? This seminar will help you think about all of that! Learn about subjects such as animal behavior, ecology, and animal-human interactions through time. There will be time at the end to ask questions, be they general or a specific idea you’re workshopping.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: Beginnings
Short Description: A dynamic board game-esque TTRPG with engaging combat, mini games, and vibrant exploration. It's lighter on the improv but still perfect for YOU to play out your ideal fantasy adventure.
Long Description: “What kind of hero will you be (insert cinematic trailer voice)?” No, seriously, what kind? There are 16 starter classes for you to choose from! Each one has its own special sauce. Like the Xiolin, who combos with up, down, left, and right basic attacks to activate unique bonus moves, just like your favorite old school fighting game. There are a variety of fun races to choose from, such as slimefolk or an awakened pot of stew, and, of course, there are the classics, including orcs, gnomes, and more. No backstory, no problem, act like yourself, and if you want to roleplay, that’s great too! You’ll be tracking down Gubba and his goblin gang. Along the way, there's a lush grassy plain to explore, full of sidequests, monsters, hidden dungeons, crafting, and minigames! You’ll definitely get distracted along the way in this lighthearted and goof-prone adventure! This is a 2 day event.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Base Like a Druid: Forests
Short Description: Learn how to make your forest-themed miniature bases and terrain more realistic through natural history. Class taught by a scientist with a background in geology, biology, and informal education.
Long Description: Are you looking to level up your miniature bases and terrain? Or only just starting to create them? If making your nature-themed pieces more realistic sounds interesting, this is the class for you! The subject of this session is forests. I’ll teach you some basic natural history as it applies to making pieces for tabletop gaming. Then we’ll make two bases together that sample different materials, methods, and habitats that fall under the session’s theme. You’ll leave the class with two completed bases, a sample kit of basing materials, new ideas, and some resources to keep exploring them.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Taily-po: An Appalachian Folktale
Short Description: There's a mountain you ought not set foot on in winter. Whether the harsh reality of nature or haunted by spooks, that place has bad juju. Your party is sent to find a fool who ignored the warnings.
Long Description:
This is a level 3 folk horror and mystery one-shot for Pathfinder 2e based on a real-world folktale I grew up with in the Southern Appalachians. It features overland travel, environmental conditions, and investigation components. CW: pet death 
 You may bring your own character or use a provided pre-gen.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Shadows over Nordvorn
Short Description: Nordvorn is gripped by strange deaths and unexplained disappearances. Animals mutilated, a young woman with an unknown malady, and whispers of a shadowy presence have spread through the city.
Long Description:
Something sinister has arrived in Nordvorn. Animals have been mutilated, citizens whisper of missing pets, a young merchant’s daughter fades with each passing day, and whispers of a shadowy presence have spread through the city. Only the cleverest and bravest will uncover the truth behind the city’s mysterious terror before something truly unspeakable occurs.
 
 An original fantasy adventure using the Dungeon Fantasy GURPS rules is part of the Nick and Nose World Tour which brings fast, welcoming GURPS games to the table wherever we roll (3d6!) 
 
 We’re dedicated to great play and may accept 1–2 walk-ups if space allows.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Jeff Wilson, Nick & Nose World Tour
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: GURPS Game Aid for Foundry
Short Description: Nick and Nose are the original developers of the GURPS Game Aid for Foundry, the best way to play GURPS online. Nick will walk you through how to set up and use GGA.
Long Description:
GURPS Game Aid for Foundry (GGA) is the only (unofficially) blessed-by-SJGames version of GURPS on FoundryVTT. In this seminar, Nick will walk you through basic set up and configuration, demonstrate compatibility with GURPS Character Sheet (GCS) and GURPS Character Assistant (GCA) character sheet programs, show how to run GURPS tactical combat, and more!
 
 Of special interest are On-the-Fly (OTF) formulas -- GGA's custom scripting language that lets nontechnical users create buttons when pressed, can execute game rolls and use the results to drive specific rules outcomes.
 
 This seminar is part of the Nick & Nose World Tour which brings fast, welcoming play to the (Virtual) Tabletop wherever we roll (3d6)!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Jeff Wilson, Nick & Nose World Tour
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Blackout
Short Description: Alone on a lonely road, the lights fail and fear rises. Something watches. Can you last the night? A rules-light modern survival horror game.
Long Description:
A violent storm strands strangers on a lonely road. Power flickers, visibility drops, and the night feels… wrong. Something unseen stalks the darkness, testing nerves and resolve. Every choice matters—do you act or hide, help others or save yourself?
 
 In this tense adventure, you are not heroes. You survive by managing fear, protecting each other, and deciding when to push your luck—and when to stay hidden. Expect claustrophobic suspense, moral dilemmas, and escalating dread in a single session designed to keep hearts racing and hands trembling.
 
 This rules-light modern survival horror GURPS game is part of the Nick and Nose (and friends!) World Tour which brings fast, welcoming GURPS games to the table wherever we roll (3d6!) 
 
 We’re dedicated to great play and may accept 1–2 walk-ups if space allows.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Jeff Wilson, Nick & Nose World Tour
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Spire of Chaos
Short Description: An archmage's tower has erupted in wild magic surges after a botched spell experiment and the resultant chaos threatens to engulf the nearby town. Can you protect them?
Long Description:
The archmage Malacar Veyne, once an advisor to kings, disappeared into his tower many years past. A few weeks ago, magic eruptions (portals, gravity changes, illusions, etc.) started occurring in and around the tower. These occurrences are getting more frequent and spreading farther, threatening to engulf the town of Tower’s Edge. The mayor has hired your team of adventurers to investigate!
 
 This adventure is an Introduction to GURPS (the Generic Universal Role Playing System). The rules will be taught to you in stages, no experience necessary.
 
 This game is part of the Nick and Nose (and friends!) World Tour which brings fast, welcoming GURPS games to the table wherever we roll (3d6!) We’re dedicated to great play and may accept 1–2 walk-ups if space allows.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Chris Normand, Nick & Nose World Tour

Title: WrastleVania: The Ultimate Dark Match
Short Description: Can you help save Greco-Romania from ULTRA DRACULA? Step up into the ring in this NO HOLDS BARRED metroidvania, wrestling, castle-crawling, TTRPG smash-up! All Round 1 Wrastlers get a free Zine!
Long Description:
Ultra Dracula has rendered all weapons useless! The International Wrastle Force has called in the world's most elite wrestlers to take him down! Powered by polymorph this legacy metroidvania ttrpg is about awesome wrestlers fighting to stop Ultra Dracula, and is filled with unlockable secrets and amazing wrestling! 
 
 In ROUND 1 players will complete to see who is strong enough, fast enough, and cleaver enough and face off against ULTRA DRACULA in the Ultimate Dark Match. Rack up points by taking on his dastardly army in the ring, discovering dark secrets, and speed running your way off the top rope and onto victory. Only the highest scoring Wrestlers will move on to ROUND 2. 
 
 Oil up, get in the ring, and kayfabe to glory!
 
 ROUND 2 will take place Saturday at 5pm.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Powered by polymorph this legacy metroidvania ttrpg is about awesome wrestlers fighting to stop Ultra Dracula, and is filled with unlockable secrets and amazing wrestling! 
 
 In ROUND 1 players will complete to see who is strong enough, fast enough, and cleaver enough and face off against ULTRA DRACULA in the Ultimate Dark Match. Rack up points by taking on his dastardly army in the ring, discovering dark secrets, and speed running your way off the top rope and onto victory. Only the highest scoring Wrestlers will move on to ROUND 2. 
 
 Oil up, get in the ring, and kayfabe to glory!
 
 ROUND 2 will take place Saturday at 5pm.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: WrastleVania: The Ultimate Dark Match - The Main Event
Short Description: Go to the mat against ULTRA DRACULA to save Greco-Romania.This is a NO HOLDS BARRED metroidvania, wrestling, castle-crawling, TTRPG smash-up! All Round 2 Wrastlers get a free hat!
Long Description:
"Ultra Dracula has rendered all weapons useless! The International Wrastle Force has called in the world's most elite wrestlers to take him down! Powered by polymorph this legacy metroidvania ttrpg is about awesome wrestlers fighting to stop Ultra Dracula, and is filled with unlockable secrets and amazing wrestling! 
 
 In ROUND 2, Wrastlers that proved themselves strong enough in Round 1 will compete against ULTRA DRACULA in the Ultimate Dark Match. Rack up points by taking on his dastardly army in the ring, discovering dark secrets, and facing the biggest heel of them all, EVIL ULTRA DRACULA! 
 
 Oil up, get in the ring, and take Ultra Dracula down for the count!"
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: Calypso's Embrace
Short Description: 7 September 1695. Pirate captain Henry Avery vanished after stealing a vast Mughal treasure, sparking the first global manhunt. You are the crew racing the East India Company to his final hideaway.
Long Description: Captain Henry Avery, known as the Arch Pirate and the King of Pirates, led a daring raid on a Grand Mughal treasure convoy bound for Mecca. He escaped with more than 600,000 pounds of treasure, becoming the most wanted man on the seas and triggering the first recorded worldwide manhunt. By 1696, Avery and the treasure vanished from all records, the pursuit officially abandoned and Avery presumed dead. Rumors persist that among the stolen riches was the relic Nizeh Seh Shakheh Poseidon, Poseidon’s Trident, an artifact said to grant power over the oceans themselves. You are the crew of the Iron Grackle. Avery’s name has faded into legend, but you know the truth. The East India Company is still searching, and you are racing them to Avery’s last known hideaway. This is a multi-table event with cross-table interaction shaping a larger shared story.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Closing the Gap: The Importance of Women in Gaming
Short Description: Women have driven innovation in gaming. This seminar highlights their impact, challenges, and why elevating women’s voices strengthens game development and player culture.
Long Description: Women have shaped the gaming world from its earliest days—designing innovative mechanics, building thriving communities, and pushing the medium forward. Yet, their work often goes unseen or undervalued. This seminar explores the vital contributions of women in gaming, both historically and today, while highlighting current challenges and opportunities for greater representation. Join us for an engaging session featuring discussion, data, and real industry stories as we examine how elevating women’s voices enriches game development, content creation, and player culture as a whole.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Ruin of the Witch Kings
Short Description: The Witch Kings destroyed the world. Now, we live in the City of Skull on the edge of the Sand Waste. Salvaging treasures to keep the last city from falling to the Darkness. Venture's get a free zine!
Long Description:
Play with Mazes Architect Patrick Lynn Clapp! 
 
 The Witch Kings destroyed the world with their war. Now, we live in the last city, on the edge of the seas of sand, salvaging treasures to keep the great City of Skull from falling to the Darkness.
 
 City of Skull is the first expansion to Mazes Fantasy Role playing game. Explore with wastes around the last city, standing in external eclipse at the edge of an ocean of sand. Discover new hazards, classes, west marching, hex crawling, find old gods and new, make kings and kill gods. 
 
 Mazes is a return to the classic sword and sorcery dungeon crawler with award-winning, yet delightfully simple modern rules. The first TTRPG to use the polymorph system, Mazes is easy to learn and teach, and ready to play without a lot of prep.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Demon Hunter Training for K POP Kids!
Short Description: Gear up. It’s time for a takedown! Come learn how to slay demons with your favorite purple haired Kpop idol!
Long Description: For anyone who doesn’t have a magical voice to protect the world from pattern-marked demons, this workshop is for you! Come learn sword fighting techniques like you see in movies from professional stage combat teachers. Suggest for ages 8+. Movement clothes required. This is a beginners intro level class on how to fight on stage, NOT a martial arts class. Join Counterfeit Combat as they teach you how to fight...for fake! We will take you through the basic skills of sword fighting such as attacks, parries, disarms and more with flair! Do no harm. Come ready to learn!

Title: Bi Planes, Zeppelins, and Jet Packs OH MY!
Short Description: The airship Samuel Clemens docks in Rio on time—engines warm, lights on, no souls aboard. Harold’s Midnight Marauders came to vacation. Instead, they’re chasing a mystery in the sky.
Long Description: It’s 1932—an age of roaring engines, brass goggles, and impossible science. In blazing Rio, whispers of vanished passengers and forbidden tech ignite a skybound mystery that could change the world. Enter the scrappy Harold’s Midnight Marauders: a daring pilot, a renowned relic hunter, a cunning femme fatale, a towering circus strongman, a grizzled Spanish-American War veteran, a brilliant mad scientist, and a mysterious occultist. Strap in—adventure lives above the clouds.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Spirits in the Mine
Short Description: The 1880s Wild West frontier town of Shade Gulch is plagued by a cursed gold mine unleashing magic. Can you lift the curse before the town is destroyed?
Long Description:
The former preacher turned outlaw, Ezekiel Crowe, robbed and murdered a local shaman, believing he had magic that would grant him riches. Ezekiel took over the local mine, in the hopes of gaining untold wealth, but in doing so he unleashed a force that has been trapped for centuries. Ever since then, weird and deadly events have been occurring around the mine. The mine owner has hired your posse to go in and remove Mr Crowe and hopefully lift the curse.
 This adventure is an Introduction to GURPS (the Generic Universal Role Playing System). The rules will be taught to you in stages, no experience necessary.
 This game is part of the Nick and Nose (and friends!) World Tour which brings fast, welcoming GURPS games to the table wherever we roll (3d6!) We’re dedicated to great play and may accept 1–2 walk-ups if space allows.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Chris Normand, Nick & Nose World Tour
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Veilbreak Protocol
Short Description: In 2035, the hi-tech company Veilbreak attempted their first zero-time transport. The experiment wasn't a complete success, and the rip in space-time is causing problems.
Long Description:
The CEO of Veilbreak promised the first instantaneous transport before the end of the year. It was aggressive; some corners had to be cut, but on Dec 30th, 2035, the first inanimate object transfer was made. There were some glitches (the test cargo arrived mangled) and unfortunately, the target portal didn’t close, and all living beings within the building were sucked into the portal, presumed dead. DCOps (Dimension Control Operations) has dispatched your team to investigate the portal and close it ASAP.
 
 This adventure is an Introduction to GURPS (the Generic Universal Role Playing System). The rules will be taught to you in stages, no experience necessary.
 
 This game is part of the Nick and Nose (and friends!) World Tour which brings fast, welcoming GURPS games to the table wherever we roll (3d6!) We’re dedicated to great play and may accept 1–2 walk-ups if space allows.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Chris Normand, Nick & Nose World Tour
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: A Distant Echo
Short Description: A distant signal is detected, sending a group of survivors into the wilds of a world moved on. This post-apoc adventure pits them against forgotten tech, dangerous terrain, and a looming threat.
Long Description:
A strange, fragmented signal crackles through an ancient radio, drawing a small band of survivors into the untamed wilds of a world that has long since moved on without them. In this GURPS post-apocalyptic adventure, players confront warped remnants of human technology, treacherous terrain reclaimed by nature, and hostile forces shaped by decay and neglect. As clues accumulate, the group uncovers a hidden, escalating threat whose consequences reach far beyond the ruins ahead. What begins as a simple investigation becomes a race against time to protect everything they call home.
 
 An original GURPS adventure set in a post-apocalyptic world of danger and wonder.
 
 This game is part of the Nick and Nose (and friends!) World Tour which brings fast, welcoming GURPS games to the table wherever we roll (3d6!) We’re dedicated to great play and may accept 1–2 walk-ups if space allows.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Jason Woollard, Nick & Nose World Tour
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Bitter Song of the Black Mangroves
Short Description: A haunting melody lures villagers to the nearby swamp. The adventurers must uncover its source, face hidden dangers, and break the curse before all is lost to The Bitter Song of the Black Mangroves.
Long Description:
In a remote village perched on the edge of a dark mangrove swamp, a haunting song has begun luring villagers to their doom, spreading fear and desperation. A group of travelers band together to uncover the source of the eerie melody, navigating treacherous waterways, natural hazards, and an unraveling mystery tied to the swamp’s ancient secrets. With the village on edge and time running out, the heroes must act quickly to end the growing curse and stop The Bitter Song of the Black Mangroves. 
 
 An original GURPS adventure set in a strange and beautiful land inspired by southeast Asian folklore. Originally run at GenCon 2025. 
 
 This game is part of the Nick and Nose (and friends!) World Tour which brings fast, welcoming GURPS games to the table wherever we roll (3d6!) We’re dedicated to great play and may accept 1–2 walk-ups if space allows.

Title: MegaNeon:RailJack

A run at 600 kph. MegaMeridian Rail is a sealed city in motion, layers of passengers, commerce, and corporate security hurtling across borders without slowing. Corridors are monitored, doors lock remotely, and electric eyes never blink. Every car has a purpose, every system a failsafe, and every mistake rumbles down the line. You’re inside a moving machine that responds to disruption with precision force. Plans will fracture, alarms will blare, and exits will vanish just when you need them most. Stay ahead of the systems, outpace security, and adapt fast: or ride the rail to the end of the line.

Title: Fear Itself: Using Horror in TTRPGs
Short Description: Have you ever tried to run a 'horror' campaign just to have it fall apart? You don't need more gore, scarier beasts, a better gaming group, or a different game system. You need this presentation.
Long Description: Fear and horror are elements that many people (players and GMs alike) hope to find in a game experience. They're also some of the hardest elements to introduce and sustain effectively. We'll discuss why horror succeeds, why it fails, and how to get it back when it does fall apart. Learn techniques and tools that you can put immediately into practice in the game or campaign you're already running. Participants receive access to a digital booklet with Horror in TTRPG best practices, tips, and tricks; a selection of ambient audio files; and access to a private Discord channel for continued discussion.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Ash and Iron Chapter 1: The Gates Remember
Short Description: A dwarven expedition enters a ruined mountain realm, confronting ancient dangers and hard truths while attempting to reclaim a long-lost homeland.
Long Description:
You are a dwarf of the old blood, descended from clans that once ruled a mountain kingdom of stone, fire, and unmatched craft. Karak Zul was not merely a home, but the heart of your people’s identity, carved by generations of labor, oath, and tradition. Its fall came not by conquest, but by catastrophe, when a reckless act deep within the mines tore open a gate to something dark and hostile. The kingdom was shattered, and its survivors were dragged into slavery in the Plane of Fire.
 
 You have escaped that fate. Now, standing once more upon the Material Plane, you return to the Zul Mountains to face whatever has claimed your ancestral halls. Ruin, ancient defenses, and lingering corruption await, but the mountain still bears your clan’s marks. Level 10 pregenerated characters are provided, using the 2014 5e rules, as you decide whether Karak Zul can truly be reclaimed.

Title: Vikings: A Winter Challenge

Long Description: Raiding, let’s face it, is a warm weather activity. Try scaring the locals into submission when you jump out of your boat and fall face-first on the ice. Better just to stay home until the Spring thaw. But Vikings at home get bored. So what harm could come from an invitation to some friendly winter competition? A feared and fearsome Jarl, his soldiers legendary for their bravery and prowess, has issued just such an invitation. There’s no way you can decline and keep your reputation as "pretty fearsome yourself," so…pack your spear and your best mead. Grab a skald to tell everyone of your victory when it’s done. And maybe find some quiet way to cheat. Because you’re also widely known to be "surprisingly clever, considering." Costumes appreciated but not required. All people are celebrated here, we have no tolerance for intolerance.

Title: Table & Field Aid Safety 101: Continuum of Harm & Prevention of Sexual Harassment/Assault
Short Description: Threat recognition training using the continuum of harm. Learn to spot behaviors that precede harassment, assault, & stalking, and how early intervention prevents escalation and protects communities.

KD is also slated to speak at the Institutes of Violence, Abuse, and Trauma international summit in March 2026

Title: Cocaine Owlbear
Short Description: You're a woodland creature who has recently gained sentience when you ingested magical cocaine from the satchel of an unlucky traveler in your forest.
Long Description: Cocaine Owlbear is an (adult), lighthearted RPG about With your newfound intelligence, you plunder the belongings of the courier you killed. You find that he or she had a complicated heist plan written on parchment and hidden in their satchel bag. But it’s not just any heist. It is a complicated heist that involves stealing more of the enchanted cocaine that has granted you sentience and made you really jittery.

Long Description: Puppet Karaoke is a relaxed, fun evening where professional puppeteers, newbies, and everyone in between can put on a show together that you won't soon forget. Come to watch or come to participate. Either way, you'll have a great time. I mean, just imagine a sandwich singing your favorite Taylor Swift song. Glorious. Long Description: Described by the press as the best hidden gem at Gen Con, you can come in your pj's, bring your favorite stuffed animal, blanket, pillow, or lovey, and settle in for a relaxing wind-down from a very busy day of gaming and con-going. Title: Puppet Open Mic

Long Description: You've got a giant desire to be on a stage--but also you've got a giant desire to not have the attention on you. We get it--that's why we've created a puppet open mic, so you can do all of the work and have the puppet get all of the glory! Try your stuff in front of an audience. Or come to be in the audience and see what happens! BYOP if you intend to perform.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: A Balefull Enigma
Short Description: A monster-focused murder mystery for level 7 characters. Interrogate witnesses, consult your monster-hunter guide, and avenge death in a not-so-sleepy town.
Long Description: You are a member of the Gallop: seasoned monster-hunters that ordinary townsfolk would best keep away from. However, when missing sheep escalate into a torn apart villager, it becomes clear that those townsfolk are way out of their league. With a provided investigation journal brimming with notes on local 'fauna,' and an assortment of weapons and potions ready to slay them, saving this town is another grueling day on the job for you. You may bring a 7th level character made using the 2024 character rules, but pregens will also be provided. Dice and pencils provided if needed. Magic items will be provided during the game.
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Magic Returned
Short Description: 50 years ago magic was erased, 10 years ago it came back but wrong. Leaders of the different factions, humans, witches, demons etc, have called a summit to create a plan forward. Who will prevail?
Long Description: Fifty years ago, magic was torn from the world in a single moment—leaving spells to rot on the tongues of witches and power to fade into myth. Then, ten years ago, it came back wrong. Magic returned without mercy or pattern. Humans woke to fire in their veins, lifelong magic users were left hollow, their gifts stripped away. Witch hunters rose again, wearing old symbols with new authority, hunting the newly gifted in the name of “balance.” Those who lost everything - turned feral and desperate, killing humans to steal the magic they believe was stolen from them first. Now power is a crime and no one knows whether magic is a blessing, a punishment, or a test the world is failing. The most powerful leaders of each faction have called a summit to see what can be done to reset the balance in our world. Costumes welcome but not required.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Kristina Moseley, Alice Crawford, Beth Allen
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Short Description: You & your misfit crew of Demon Hunters must save the world from the forces of Evil. But this time, you get to bring along some of the cast from the movies. Yeah, we're screwed...
Long Description:
Come and join us for this special one-off event! Nearly 30 years ago the original horror-comedy film "Demon Hunters (1999)" introduced the world to what would eventually become Dead Gentlemen Productions and Zombie Orpheus Entertainment - this is your chance to be a part of that history. You'll get 4 hours of RPG play led by ZOE's own James "GM Jams" Durham with fan favorites Christian Doyle and - making his Gen Con debut - Steve Payne (Wolf from Demon Hunters and "The one who gets his pants stolen" from the original The Gamers).
 
 Using the Fate Accelerated system of play, the DHRPG is a fast and funny RPG experience you don't want to miss. In a world where everything and anything is real (Demons, the Tooth Fairy, Sentient Manbuns - you name it, we've defeated it), the Brotherhood of the Celestial Torch fights for truth, justice, and their next coffee break.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: Inclusion in Puppetry
Short Description: Join Puppeteers from the BIPOC and Disability communities as we discuss the highs and lows of what it means to be a puppeteer from a different culture or living with a disability.
Long Description: While puppetry is a beautiful and inclusive artistry, in recent years, the push for visible and invisible diversity and is at an all time high. This panel is a safe space to discuss what progress the puppetry community has made in making POCs (Puppeteers of Color) & people with disabilities feel comfortable in creative spaces, as well as what the community needs to work on to make it that much more inviting to those who feel outnumbered in the room where it happens.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Long Description: Choreography is very important in puppetry, especially when it comes to musical numbers and group scenes. But how do you make your puppets dance without flailing them around? Title: Wedding at Cobvale Keep
Short Description: You are aspiring knights, venturing into the wild reaches of the realm on a quest to prove yourselves worthy. What wrongs will you right and lessons will you learn along the way?
Long Description:
A PbtA game about knightly quests and coming of age, inspired by Arthurian legends, fairy tales, Tamora Pierce's Tortall books, and Magic: The Gathering's plane of Eldraine. The game is in-development from Cloven Pine Games. It uses modular playbooks where each character is created by combining two playbooks that form two halves of a heraldic shield, e.g. The Stag and The Anvil, or The Fox and The Oak.
 
 In this scenario, the Knight-Aspirants are called upon to ensure all goes well with a wedding meant to unite two formerly feuding families. As you discover dark secrets and fend off the restless dead, will you still want the wedding to go forward?
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

In Dust & Devils, loyalty is optional, explosions are inevitable, and tiny scaly legends are born in smoke and fire.

Title: Women & Femme-Aligned D&D DM 101 Class
Short Description: Learn to DM with the Ladies of D&D San Diego! This introductory workshop will give you the tools and confidence boost you need to DM your own D&D game. Women and femme-aligned participants welcome.
Long Description:
Have you always wanted to run your own D&D game, but felt too intimidated, nervous, or overwhelmed? Come banish those doubts at the Ladies of D&D San Diego (LDDSD) DM 101 Workshop! Our all-woman team has designed an introductory crash-course to give aspiring women and femme-aligned DMs all the tips, tricks, and tools to jump into your own D&D game. 
 
 As a volunteer-run community group, LDDSD has built a robust and flourishing network of lady DMs, game writers, and storytellers. We bring those lessons to you with a focus on making DMing accessible, flexible, and achievable. Our DM 101 Workshop is a comfortable, low-pressure, high-support environment where it's fun to try and safe to fail. Yes, you can DM a D&D game, and we'll prove it to you!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Women & Femme-Aligned D&D DM 102 Class
Short Description: Eager to hone your technique? Join the Ladies of D&D San Diego for an in-depth DM workshop, where you can learn to run and design a game with confidence.
Long Description:
Do you dream of running a D&D game that feels exciting, immersive, and collaborative? Are you ready to knock down the barriers holding you back? Then come join the Ladies of D&D San Diego (LDDSD) for our intensive DM 102 Workshop! 
 
 This in-depth workshop is designed for women and femme-aligned aspiring DMs, featuring an all-woman team of DMs, storytellers, and game writers who have spent years cooking up the special sauce that makes for a great DMing experience. We'll explore different techniques, tools, and approaches to creating smooth and dynamic combat, engaging roleplay, and intriguing worldbuilding. Learn DMing in a low-pressure environment of supportive peers, where its easy to succeed and just as fun to fail. Attend both our 102 and 101 workshops for the full suite of DM how-to!
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)

 This is a game focusing on the role play of the characters as they take on a hostile environment under heroic conditions. Recently revived after Ragnarök, Baldur needs heroes to secure the region and find resources to make his city beautiful.

Title: What Will Conventions Look Like in 5 Years?
Short Description: Join gaming historian Matt Shoemaker and the hosts of All Year I Dream About Gaming Conventions Quarex and Ben as they entertain you with speculation on the future of gaming conventions.
Long Description:
Tabletop gaming conventions are consistently growing in both size and popularity. In the wake of the pandemic, existing conventions have seen record numbers of attendees, while more and more conventions are being founded to meet gaming demand and bridge geographic divides. 
 
 
 How will conventions manage this continued growth over the next five years? Which conventions will become or remain the most popular? What should the behemoths like Gen Con, Dragon Con, and PAX Unplugged do to remain on their pedestal? How might tabletop gaming at conventions change after the next few years? What trends will emerge? 
 
 Join Matt Shoemaker, gaming historian and author of the book Gathering of Gamers, and Quarex and Ben, the hosts of the podcast All Year I Dream About Gaming Conventions, as they entertain you with speculation on the future of gaming conventions.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Matt Shoemaker, Andrew "Quarex" Huntleigh, Benjamin Eastman
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Sickness of Hive Willowbottom
Short Description: A strange disease has stricken your fellow bees and you are tasked with finding the cure. Play as groups of Super Sisters, bees with a strong genetic bond, for the good of the hive.
Long Description:
The hive is in danger! A strange disease has stricken your fellow bees, and you are tasked with finding the cure. Play this new game by acting as a group of Super Sisters, bees with a strong genetic bond, as you work with other players' bees for the good of the hive. This game is designed to get you to think as a bee, not an individual, so be prepared to explore role-playing games with a new buzz.
 
 This game takes inspiration from titles such as Bunnies & Burrows where players interact with the world as naturalistic versions of animals. In this case, honey bees! Bee lives are complex, and life is cheap. In this game you will see what it is like to put your collective first, and do whatever it takes to make sure that your Queen and your sisters survive. Even if it means your own life. Can you successfully cure the disease that has been spreading in the hive, or will you all perish?
Game System: For the Good of the Hive
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Materials Required Details: A set of standard polyhedral dice
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Murder Hornets Attack Hive Willowbottom
Short Description: You are used to wasps and hornets, but these bugs are different. They've been terrorizing the neighborhood and your queen is worried your hive is next. How will you deal with this emerging threat?
Long Description:
The bees of Hive Willowbottom are no stranger to wasps and hornets, but this season something is different. Larger bugs with a thicker, seemingly impenetrable, carapace have begun are in area. Neighboring Hive Coneflower has recently gone silent and all the buzz is that these new interlopers are responsible. What will you do to ensure your hive can survive if these giants attack?
 
 This game takes inspiration from titles like Bunnies & Burrows, where players interact with the world as naturalistic versions of animals. In this case, honey bees! Bee lives are complex, and life is cheap. In this game you will put your collective first, and do whatever it takes to make sure that your Queen and your sisters survive. Even if it means your own life. Can you successfully unlock the secrets of the Murder Hornets in time, or will you all perish?
Game System: For the Good of the Hive

Short Description: February 1968: Delta Green is dispatched to Cambodia to track down a French occultist’s cache of artifacts. The agents are to secure the assets and eliminate the occultist, with extreme prejudice.
Long Description: With the inauguration of Nixon and the beginning of the Khmer Rouge insurgency, agents are tasked with locating a cache of artifacts and tomes owned by a French occultist, Severin Hamon. Not knowing whether he’s dead or alive, the agents must find him before the political situation spirals out of control or the assets are collected by someone else. Title: Colossal Undertaking
Short Description: The shade of King Tarama cannot rest until he is buried in his ancestral home. The kingdom’s greatest heroes will be needed to brave the void and conquer the colossus that the land itself has become.
Long Description: Untold aeons ago, the world shattered, leaving behind fragments of reality in a sea of nothingness. Those creatures who are left, draw upon the power of Anima, the essence of being itself, to overcome the forces of Aether, its destroying opposite. It is this Aether, in the form of a continent-sized colossus, that has swallowed the once-great kingdom of Tirtha. The death of humankind’s greatest ruler, King Tarama, has spurred his people to find this terrible beast and free their ancestral home so that it may be part of the world once more. Only once Tirtha is again the realm of humanity can the dead king be laid to rest, and the struggle of existence continue.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Christina Noel, Kevin Noel, Lisa Purdin, Daniel Zeiger
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Improv for RPGers
Short Description: Stop freezing or blocking. Start playing. Players and GMs will learn practical tools they can use immediately. Build better scenes, share the glory, and keep games moving when nothing goes to plan.
Long Description: Tabletop roleplaying runs on collaboration, surprise, and shared discovery—but those skills don’t always step onstage when the dice hit the table. This interactive workshop introduces practical improv tools designed specifically for TTRPG play. Explore how players and GMs can support each other as an ensemble, respond confidently to unexpected choices, and keep scenes moving without losing tone or momentum. Learn how to accept and shape offers, share the spotlight, use status to energize roleplay, and recognize habits that quietly stall cooperative storytelling. Whether you’ve frozen mid-scene, blocked an idea without meaning to, or felt stuck waiting in the wings, this class gives you tools you can use immediately. Expect discussion, examples, and hands-on exercises focused on building stronger scenes, smoother play, and moments that earn a well-deserved standing ovation at your table.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Secrets of Board Game Manufacturing
Short Description: From blueprint to board: Uncover the journey of game manufacturing. Learn key steps, cost-quality tips & see exclusive videos. Essential for creators. Don't miss the secrets!
Long Description:
Ever wondered how a board game goes from concept to your table? Join us to uncover the complete manufacturing story!
 
 We’ll reveal each major production step, share pro tips for maximizing quality and managing costs, and show exclusive live-commentary videos of key components being produced.
 
 Whether you're a publisher, designer, or simply passionate about games, this talk will equip you with the insights needed to turn your vision into reality. Don't miss out on the secrets behind crafting great games!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Blending BootCamp
Short Description: Learn three different methods of creating smooth color transitions on your model with master miniature painter Matt DiPietro. Try each method and discover the right blending method for you!
Long Description: Join master miniature painter Matt DiPietro for a comprehensive class that is part demonstration and part hands-on experience. Matt will demonstrate glaze blending, two-brush blending, and wet blending while discussing the advantages and disadvantages of each method. In the hands-on portion, you will practice a blending method of your choice with Matt there to provide you with coaching to ensure your success. Practice models are provided and are yours to keep. A selection of paints will be provided for students to use. Students may want to bring their own brushes or other preferred materials.
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Materials Required Details: Paint brushes
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Sketch Style: Speed Painting for Gaming & Beyond
Short Description: Achieve bold, expressive painting in a minimum amount of time! This style is a great way to visualize lighting. Join Matt DiPietro as he paints a model from start to finish in an hour.
Long Description: Sketch style is based on a centuries-old, simple and efficient method of fine art painting called “grisaille.” This style is a great way to visualize lighting, object-source lighting, and non-metallic metals. The method is more than just an automatic self working technique. You will learn to paint models quickly in a style that trains you to become a better future painter. Open up the door to display painting while getting your models ready for the table top!
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Materials Required Details: Paint brushes
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Raid on Castle Raisinloaf - Midnight Sun
Short Description: Homeless kobolds raid Castle Raisinloaf to seize Vampire Lord C'hahd's hidden Heart. Win a new home. Fail and become dinner. Chaos, guns, and magipunk mayhem!
Long Description:
Special Horror Edition!
 
 In Kobold Quest: Dust & Devils, a narrative-driven, wild west, steampunk magipunk adventure bursting with chaos, light rules, and ridiculous fun, your homeless, hungry kobold clan is one bad torch away from extinction—and desperate times call for reckless real estate acquisition. The plan? Seize Castle Raisinloaf and make it yours.
 
 Unfortunately, it’s occupied by the brooding (and extremely bitey) Vampire Lord C'hahd and a buffet of monsters, mercenaries, and things that skitter in the dark. Rumor whispers his still-beating Heart lies hidden deep within the castle’s cursed halls. Capture it, and the pale tyrant must kneel. Fail, and you’re appetizers. Grab your six-shooters, your posse, and your questionable courage—tonight, the kobolds raid.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Cinder & Ash
Short Description: Chaotic kobolds race a dragon and a devil-bound tyrant to steal an ancient relic. Cut deals, spark mayhem, and betray everyone before the town burns to ash.
Long Description:
Kobold Quest: Dust & Devils rides again in a blaze of magipunk mayhem! A dying frontier town hides an ancient relic humming with forbidden power—and every monster with ambition can smell it. Cinder, a fire-blooded dragon, wants it to rule the wastes. Lord Ashcroft, a silver-tongued tyrant, plans to crack it open and ascend as an archfiend. And your band of reckless kobolds? You’re here to steal it first.
 
 Roam dusty streets, spin rumors into leverage, duel rival gangs, rig contraptions, and cut deals that will absolutely backfire. Side with dragonfire or deviltry… or play them both. The cards drive the chaos, the story bends to your choices, and the clock is ticking. In Dust & Devils, loyalty is optional, explosions are inevitable, and tiny scaly legends are born in smoke and fire.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Whispers from the Deep
Short Description: Devil’s Gulch is silent. Team up with 6 kobolds to uncover the mystery, grab the Mind Shard, and escape “alive” in this high-octane magipunk heist.
Long Description:
Kobold Quest: Dust & Devils is a high-octane, improv-driven, rules-light, card-powered narrative heist set in a lawless western steampunk magipunk frontier. Devil’s Gulch has gone dark. Travelers vanish. The wildlife has fled. Vultures circle above an empty town that should not be empty.
 
 Gather up to six other kobolds and venture into the silent streets to uncover the truth, seize the mysterious Mind Shard, and escape… “alive.” This two-hour, loosely cooperative chaos-fest rewards bold creativity, reckless teamwork, and increasingly catastrophic decisions as you break in, grab the prize, and get out — preferably before everything (and everyone) explodes.
 
 What is the Mind Shard? Why do you need it? How many kobolds can you fit in a trench coat? Only time will tell.
 
 Presented by Kobold Game Labs — please use your Kobold Posse Responsibly.
 Adventure Track 2 (2026)
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Ember Event
Short Description: Trapped in a deadly time loop, you and up to 6 kobolds must unravel the Time Shard’s mystery and stop an arcane cataclysm in this fast-paced, chaotic magipunk adventure.
Long Description:
You awaken with a box. A single glowing rune burns across its surface. You step into town - moments later, the sky tears open in an arcane cataclysm. Everyone dies. You awaken again. The box remains. Now two runes glow.
 
 Kobold Quest: Dust & Devils is a high-octane, improv-driven, rules-light, card-powered narrative adventure set in a lawless western steampunk magipunk frontier. Trapped in a deadly time loop, you and up to six other kobolds must explore the town, unravel the mystery of the Time Shard, and break the cycle before the world ends - again.
 
 This two-hour, loosely cooperative chaos-fest rewards bold creativity, reckless teamwork, and increasingly catastrophic decisions as you experiment, adapt, and desperately try to escape… “alive.”
 
 What is the Time Shard? Why is it bound to you? How many kobolds can you fit in a trench coat?
 
 Only time will tell.
 Track 3 (2026)

Title: Dead Channel in Neon Sector
Short Description: Street operators take a high-risk job in a collapsing megacity where tech fails, bullets don’t, and escape is never clean.
Long Description:
The city is broken, the lights are still on, and someone is about to get paid.
 
 This is a fast, brutal one-session dive into neon-lit chaos and bad decisions. Characters are fragile, violence is sudden, and implants fail at the worst possible time. Expect tense negotiations, dirty hacks, street-level firefights, and the constant pressure of being hunted by forces with better gear and fewer morals. Every move closes one door and opens a worse one.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: The Sepulcher of Ash and Teeth
Short Description: A doomed expedition into a blighted ruin where something ancient refuses to stay buried. Expect rot, prophecy, sudden violence, and a short, brutal struggle against the end of the world.
Long Description:
The world is ending and you are already too late.
 In The Sepulcher of Ash and Teeth, the dying lead you into a cursed ruin choked with soot, bones, and bad prophecy. Something inside refuses to stay buried, and every step closer makes the air thicker, the omens worse, and the choices more final.
 
 This is a fast, brutal one-session descent into rot and desperation. Characters are fragile, violence is sudden, and mercy is rare. Expect collapsing stone, blasphemous relics, malformed dead, and a creeping sense that the dungeon itself wants you gone. There are no heroes here—only scavengers, sinners, and fools trying to survive long enough to see what kills them.
 
 The tone is bleak, violent, and darkly funny. The rules are simple, the danger is constant, and the end is always near. Bring dice, make bad choices, and see how long you last before the world closes in.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Long Description:
The city is broken, the lights are still on, and someone is about to get paid.
 In this cyberpunk adventure, you are desperate operators pulled into a high-risk job in a collapsing megacity ruled by corporate violence, failing infrastructure, and disposable people. What starts as a routine score spirals fast as security tightens, tech glitches, and the situation turns hostile from every angle.
 
 This is a fast, brutal one-session dive into neon-lit chaos and bad decisions. Characters are fragile, violence is sudden, and implants fail at the worst possible time. Expect tense negotiations, dirty hacks, street-level firefights, and the constant pressure of being hunted by forces with better gear and fewer morals. Every move closes one door and opens a worse one.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Devil’s Due at Broken Keel Bay
Short Description: Desperate pirates hunt cursed plunder through storms, mutiny, and blood-soaked decks. Survival not guaranteed.
Long Description: This is a fast, brutal one-session descent into salt, blood, and betrayal. Characters are fragile, violence is sudden, and mercy is a liability. Expect boarding actions, cannon fire, rusted blades, mutiny, and the constant threat of drowning. Every choice makes things worse, and the sea does not care who you are or what you believe.
