Title: The Sepulcher of Ash and Teeth
Short Description: A doomed expedition into a blighted ruin where something ancient refuses to stay buried. Expect rot, prophecy, sudden violence, and a short, brutal struggle against the end of the world.
Long Description:
The world is ending and you are already too late.
 In The Sepulcher of Ash and Teeth, the dying lead you into a cursed ruin choked with soot, bones, and bad prophecy. Something inside refuses to stay buried, and every step closer makes the air thicker, the omens worse, and the choices more final.
 
 This is a fast, brutal one-session descent into rot and desperation. Characters are fragile, violence is sudden, and mercy is rare. Expect collapsing stone, blasphemous relics, malformed dead, and a creeping sense that the dungeon itself wants you gone. There are no heroes here—only scavengers, sinners, and fools trying to survive long enough to see what kills them.
 
 The tone is bleak, violent, and darkly funny. The rules are simple, the danger is constant, and the end is always near. Bring dice, make bad choices, and see how long you last before the world closes in.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Dog Eat Dog
Short Description: Come play Dog Eat Dog: A Game of Imperialism and Assimilation on the Pacific Islands.
Long Description: Dog Eat Dog is a role-playing game of colonialism and its consequences. As a group, players work together to describe the conquest of one of the hundreds of small islands in the Pacific Ocean, defining the customs of the native people and the mores of the outsiders arriving to claim it. One player then assumes the role of the Occupation force, playing their capable military, their collaborationist government, and whatever jaded tourists and shrewd businessmen are interested in this not-quite-pacified territory. All the others play individual Natives, each trying in their own ways to come to terms with the new regime.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Monumental Consequence
Short Description: In this game, you will become villagers in the occupied town of La Ville. You face an impossible choice: destroy your centuries-old church to save lives, or risk everything to preserve the art within.
Long Description: Monumental Consequence is part social deduction game and part parlor LARP. The game plays in 30-45 minutes, during which your character needs to decide if art is worth dying for.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: Keep the Faith
Short Description: Build a religion and watch it fracture in this storytelling game about faith in transition.
Long Description: Keep the Faith is a storytelling game of belief, tension, and transformation, designed to create compelling narratives and spark discussions between players. Players become members of a fictional Faith in transition, shaping its rituals, symbols, and stories as beliefs evolve and fracture under pressure. Through cards and shared narration, they explore how conviction, community, and power shape a religion’s future.

Title: Beginner Ballroom Dance: Shim Sham
Short Description: Did you know that we do line dances in the ballroom, too! Some are solo versions of partner dances, and others are traditional line dances we just decided were fun! Come learn a few!
Long Description: Note that this class is available in several sessions, each of which focuses on a different dance. This session will be covering the Shim Sham: a solo jazz line dance. The Shim Sham is a staple first line dance for swing dancers, with regional variations danced in almost every swing dance/lindy hop community around the world. We’ll be teaching a beginner version of the routine, while showing a few variations for anyone who wants to try a little bit more. Though this workshop is beginner-friendly, it may be helpful to take one of our other classes first, like swing or west coast.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Alexander Jacobs, Terri Ann Guingab, Stephanie Tompkins, Ike Elele
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Invitation to the Ball: A History of Ballroom Dance & Culture
Short Description: The masquerade ball is a classic trope of film & fantasy. Learn the culture and find out what makes a waltz Viennese, how to behave when the Duke asks you to dance, and why vampires love tango.
Long Description: The masquerade ball is a classic trope not only of gaming but of fantasy and adventure literature in general. Come learn what actually happens at a ball, the history of the dances, and the culture surrounding dance. Understand what makes a waltz “Viennese,” how you should behave when the Duke asks you to dance, and why a vampire would tango, and maybe even polka, but never samba. This seminar will inform you on the history of ballroom dance and include a discussion about how to apply it in your games and fiction.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: As You Wave Me Goodbye
Short Description: An unusual project for the team from Smythe's Discreet Investigations -- a swimmer attempting a Channel crossing seems to be concerned about more than weather, currents and tides.
Long Description: To swim the English Channel is regarded as an astounding feat of endurance -- and no woman has yet managed it. The renowned American swimmer Gertie Eberhard plans to attempt the crossing in August 1926. She and her team are acutely aware that, of the two women known to have tried the journey before, one was never seen again and the other has mysteriously faded from public view. Eberhard has had Smythe's Discreet Investigations, London's first -- indeed, only -- all-female detective agency recommended to her, and has engaged the team to manage her security before and during the swim.
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Beavertown Bag Job
Short Description: A data theft job gone wrong. One of your crew is already dead & so is the homeowner. Emergency services are en route. You gotta run, NOW.
Long Description:
A simple data snatch in a quiet corpo suburb goes sideways fast. Your netrunner fries when the alarm screams, the homeowner’s flatlined, and Trauma Team is inbound. Local security is locking down the block, your fixer’s ghosting you, and the ride you came in is parked way down the street. You wanted to make a name in Night City—grab your iron, choom. Time to earn it.
 This is unofficial content provided under the Homebrew Content Policy of R. Talsorian Games and is not approved or endorsed by RTG. This content references materials that are the property of R. Talsorian Games and its licenses.

Title: Collaborative Worldbuilding in TTRPGs
Short Description: Join Darrington Press’s Daggerheart devs Spenser Starke, Rowan Hall, and Elise Rezendes along with special guests for a panel on the ins and outs of worldbuilding.
Long Description: Players and their characters always leave their mark on the worlds they play in, but building the world has traditionally fallen to the role of game masters. Many modern TTRPGs encourage and provide frameworks for sharing worldbuilding with the players, which can heighten player investment and an interweaving of the characters and the world they’re in. In this panel, we’ll cover best practices of incorporating players into the worldbuilding process.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: So You Want to Paint a Dragon
Short Description: In this class you will learn how to paint the most definitive fantasy creature: a dragon! Discover how to use color, texture, and light on a fun model in a relaxed environment!
Long Description: Do you want to paint a dragon? Is that even a question?! Of course you do! And that means this is the class for you! In this class we will learn practical and fun ways to use color, texture, and light on a very forgiving subject. Watch your imagination come alive in a low-stakes environment! Each student will receive a class bust designed by @abyssoul_fp. Some paints and brushes will be available for use, but feel welcome to bring your own.

Title: Dead in Thay : Multi-Table Persistent Epic - Track A
Short Description: ​4 parties, 1 mission: Conquer a 100+ room dungeon! Successes affect all tables in this D&D 5.5e multi-table epic. Persistent play: return daily to finish the siege. Join the strike on Thay!
Long Description:
To play the full campaign, please purchase tickets for the same time slot on Thursday, Friday, Saturday, & Sunday Track A Part 1 of 4.
 
 The Lich Lord Szass Tam has built a nightmare—The Doomvault—& it will take more than one party to bring it down. In this "Round Table Games" exclusive, four adventuring parties will operate in the same dungeon simultaneously, sharing a common goal: the Bloodgate Nexus.
 
 While each table explores independently, your successes (& failures) affect the overall war effort. We are using the D&D 5.5e Rules for a fresh, tactical experience.
 
 This is a persistent event. We highly encourage you to book the same time slot for Thursday, Friday, Saturday, & Sunday. You will play the same character, retain your level-ups, and see the campaign through to the final epic conclusion. High-quality physical props, professional GMs, and a unified war-map included!
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Beginner’s Miniature Painting Workshop - With Gnomes
Short Description: Do you want to paint miniatures but don’t know where to start? This workshop will cover all you need to know to get started and then paint your first (and maybe second) Miniature!
Long Description:
This introductory workshop is designed for those new to the miniature painting hobby and will provide participants with a solid foundation in the fundamentals of the craft. Attendees will learn:
 ● The essential tools and materials required for miniature painting
 ● Best practices for model preparation, including cleaning and priming
 ● Core painting techniques that establish a strong base for future skill development
 Each participant will receive:
 ● High-quality paints supplied by Monument Hobbies
 ● Professional-grade brushes courtesy of Chronicle RPGs
 ● A gnome miniature by Warp Miniatures to serve as the class model
 By the end of the workshop, participants will have completed their first painted miniature and gained the knowledge necessary to confidently continue exploring the hobby.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Dice Throne: Translating a Physical Game to Digital Without Losing the Table Feel
Short Description: Explore the creative and technical decisions involved in adapting a beloved tabletop game for digital platforms while maintaining its feel and core player experience.
Long Description: Join Nate Chatellier, cofounder and lead game designer of Dice Throne and CEO of Nerd Ninjas, for a behind-the-scenes look at the process of adapting a tabletop game for digital platforms. This seminar explores the design and development choices behind Dice Throne Digital, the upcoming digital adaptation of the tabletop game Dice Throne, from core systems and rules implementation to interface design and platform-specific considerations. Nate will share challenges encountered, lessons learned, and the team’s approach to building a faithful digital experience across iOS, Android, Steam, and Meta Quest 3/3s (mixed reality). Attendees will learn the practical realities of translating physical games into digital form while preserving the feel and core player experience.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Nate Chatellier, Jondi Soper, Chris Davis
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Quantum Dallas
Short Description: A session of the baffling 1979 officially licensed RPG based on the 1978 soap opera “Dallas” with a bizarre sci-fi twist. You don’t need to know anything about Dallas to play.
Long Description: While embroiled in their regular scheming and backstabbing, the oil magnates of the Ewing family discover their land is also home to an interdimensional portal connecting them to parallel universes. If there is one thing a true Ewing knows, it’s that where there are deals to be made, there is money and power to follow. Players take on the role of characters from Dallas split across many worlds. To start, you will be divided into groups in separate universes where your characters have a tangled web of power, relationships, and goals. Each universe will have a sci-fi tongue-in-cheek quirk that layers an outlandish sci-fi quirk onto the usual Dallas melodrama. Then a portal will open, allowing characters to hop between worlds, causing chaos and making their goals possible in new ways. We’re using the basic Dallas system with a few original mechanics to accommodate this strange premise.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: GM Workshop: Dynamic Combat

Title: D&D Adventurers League: The Sage of Cormanthor
Short Description: As the grain rots, the town of Elmwood hires you to seek out the Sage of Cormanthor's wisdom. A D&D Adventurers League adventure for levels 5-10.
Long Description: The grain has rotted in the town of Elmwood, which means people might starve, businesses and banks might fail, and endanger the town’s already-precarious existence. Brave adventurers are needed to travel through the wild forest to seek the advice of the Sage of Cormanthor. D&D Adventurers League. New to Adventurers League? We will have some level 5 pregen characters available, or you are also welcome to create your own level 5 character using the Adventurers League organized play guides, which can be found on our website (https://atxal.com/GenCon). Message the GM if you need assistance. CCC-BMG-16 The Sage of Cormanthor. A four-hour Adventurers League adventure for Tier 2 Characters (levels 5-10).
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)

Title: D&D Adventurers League: A Martyred Heart
Short Description: In the wake of an assassination, a priest of Ilmater stands trial. But is this really the truth of the matter, or is it fate? A beginner-friendly D&D AL adventure for levels 1-4.
Long Description: As Thentia’s lighthouse grows dim, so too does the faith within the city, as various powers wage a quiet struggle in the wake of an assassination. In the midst of this, a priest of Ilmater has been accused of a heinous crime. Is this all really the truth of the matter, or are we all just Agents of Fate? New to D&D or Adventurers League? Level 1 pregen characters will be available, or you are welcome to create your own character using the Adventurers League organized play guides, which can be found on our website. Have your own Tier 1 AL character already? Great! This module is fun for all levels of D&D and AL experience. CCC-TAROT-01-03 A Martyred Heart. A beginner-friendly Adventurers League adventure for levels 1-4.

Title: D&D Adventurers League: The Lost Apprentice
Short Description: A young apprentice to the Council of Mages has not reported back for several weeks. Is he simply late or is there something more sinister afoot? A beginner-friendly D&D AL adventure for levels 1-4.

Title: D&D Adventurers League: Tomb of Horrors - Mega Dungeon Crawl!
Short Description: The sinister Tomb of Horrors is a labyrinthine crypt, with terrible traps, ferocious monsters, and magical treasures...all under the eye of evil lich Acererak. A D&D AL adventure for levels 11-16.
Long Description: Before there was much else in the world of D&D, there was the Tomb of Horrors! The first version of the adventure was crafted for Gary Gygax’s personal campaign in the early '70s and went on to be featured as the official D&D event at the original Origins gaming convention in 1975. The first publication of Tomb of Horrors, as a part of the Advanced D&D game, came in 1978. As a proving ground for characters and players alike, fabricated by the devious mind of the game’s co-creator, Tomb of Horrors has no equal in the annals of D&D’s greatest adventures. Only high-level characters stand a chance of coming back alive, but every player who braves the Tomb will have the experience of a lifetime. To participate in this module, you must have an existing AL character in Tier 3 (levels 11-16). DDHC-TYP-7 Tomb of Horrors. A D&D Adventurers League adventure for levels 11-16.
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)

Stuffed full of mechanical traps and arcane wards, and rumored to be inhabited by fiends, very few have managed to extract any REAL treasure from the former home of Durlag Trollkiller. After a pleasant tea in her garden, SEER calls upon you to beat him there and put a stop to his efforts! Have your own AL character already? Great! This module is fun for all levels of D&D and AL experience. CONTENT WARNING: This adventure contains themes of Cute Goblin Child Endangerment (Mention), Torture (reference), Unexpected Parenthood. Player discretion is advised. Title: FIASCO LIVE! (General Admission Ticket)
Short Description: An all-star cast of L.A. improv comedians hilariously implode in the stage adaptation of FIASCO LIVE! Expect schemes, laughs, delusions, and probably murder. Nothing ends well—that's the fun!
Long Description: An all-star cast of improv comedians brings the award-winning card game FIASCO to life on stage. Watch schemes, betrayals, and catastrophically bad decisions in Poppleton, U.S.A.—the perfect small town for greed, lust, and envy to collide. Our cast: AMY VORPAHL (Dropout, Nerdist, Buzzfeed), DAVE HILL (iO, Modern Family, Hitman), and special guests from Dropout, TV appearances, and the gaming world. Inspired by cinematic capers where desperate people make terrible choices, this fully improvised show guarantees one thing: chaos. Our delusional, twisted characters don't stand a chance—but you'll laugh at every doomed decision they make. From ill-conceived heists to poorly-thought-out affairs, watch the dominoes fall in real-time as our performers navigate the glorious trainwreck together. Nothing ends well and that's the whole point. (Cast subject to change—but the disaster is guaranteed.)

Title: D&D Adventurers League: The Hystero
Short Description: Missing supply shipments need investigating, but there is something else, like an itch you can’t scratch, a splinter in your mind, bothering you. A D&D Adventurers League adventure for levels 5-10.
Long Description: *Author-run adventure* After many adventures, you have grown in strength and renown across Faerûn, but lately something just isn’t right. You are happy, wealthy, and content but there something else, like an itch you can’t scratch, or a splinter in your mind that you just can’t reach. Your most recent adventure has you headed to the outskirts of the city of Waterdeep to investigate some missing people and supply shipments for a local merchant, but you can’t help that nagging feeling in the back of your mind that something just feels off. Will you figure out what is going on with the missing shipments and what nags in the deep recesses of your mind? CONTENT WARNING: Blood, Claustrophobia, Possession, Death, Dismemberment, Parasites, Loss of Self, Tentacles, Mind control. SJ-DC-HYS-01 The Hystero. A 4-Hour D&D Adventurers League adventure for Tier 2 Characters (levels 5-10).
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)

Title: So You Want to Build a Circulating Board Game Collection
Short Description: What information do you need to pitch a circulating board game collection to your library? What comes after the yes? Cataloger Matthew Scrivner explores the answers to these questions and more.
Long Description: What strategies convince Collection Development departments or other internal stakeholders to take a chance on a gaming collection? If you get the yes, what comes next? Speaking from his own experience, Kansas City Public Library Cataloger Matthew Scrivner will share ideas and key points in building a pitch for Collections departments and administrators; meaningful ways to explore the work of other public libraries; how to build support across departments; basics of cataloging games for catalog discovery; and ways to develop processing practices that are sustainable through the lenses of time management and finances.

Title: Get in the Game (at the Library!)
Short Description: Learn how to build engaging all-ages programming for your library. Gain practical advice on how to choose the best games for your collection.
Long Description: Are you a library employee looking to start a gaming program? Or maybe a library user who wants to help their library start gaming? Explore all the interesting side quests that tabletop gaming can take in the library from teen internships to creating a reference gaming website to building community partnerships. We will offer effective strategies and examples of board game facilitation best practices. The audience will learn how to create a professional development cohort through gaming. We will also explore selling the idea to library management and collection development strategies for all ages, including practical tips.

Title: The Undead of Brackhill Citadel
Short Description: Legions of undead rise from their graves and march towards the mountain garrison. Many have investigated its cause, but none have returned. Will you?
Long Description:
Set in the small town of Brosa (homebrew), the citizens of this small town have lived under the shadow of the mountain garrison, Brackhill Citadel. For centuries, the lords who called the citadel home lived in peace with the residents of the mountain village…until now. Recently, the dead have been called from their graves and have been marching towards the garrison in numbers that suggest an army is brewing. 
 
 This game will be a dungeon crawl, with roleplay, puzzles, and a whole bunch of monsters. You can expect horror and terror lurking in the dark, and be presented with difficult choices all along the way. This is a horror game, with themes involving death, gore, body horror, and some intense descriptions of violence. 
 
 Will you be able to solve the mystery of Brackhill, or will your bodies join the army of the undead?
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Henna 101
Short Description: Gain supplies and learn techniques of applying and maintaining henna when applied to skin.
Long Description: Henna/Mehndi temporary tattoos have deep symbolism and rich history from Ancient Egypt to current-day India. In this class you will learn what ingredients go into the paste and practice using pre-made cones free-hand and with stencils on both paper and skin. Summer will explain basic tips for deeper coloration, care, and designs. You will get two full-sized cones, an applicator bottle, stencils and info packet. There will be plenty of time for practice, application, and leftover time to sit at the table and let the henna dry/set. This will leave a TEMPORARY tattoo, lasting 3 days-2 weeks. If you have skin allergies, disease, or discomfort on your hands or arms, this is not the class for you.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Don't Whistle in the Mountains
Short Description: Fog-choked roads, lonely rail lines, and a haunting whistle in the mountains. In the Greenbrier Valley, following the sound means vanishing--unless you uncover what's calling from the mist.
Long Description:
Greenbrier Valley is wrapped in fog and old stories. Abandoned rail lines cut through dense forest, river mist rolls in against the wind, and locals know better than to travel alone after dark. When people begin vanishing along back roads and forgotten tunnels, the only clue left behind is a long, echoing whistling drifting through the trees. 
 
 This Monster of the Week one-shot is a slow-burn Appalachian horror mystery focused on investigation, atmosphere, and creeping dread. Expect missing persons, unreliable witnesses, and folklore passed down in half-whispered warnings. The thread doesn't chase--it lures. Sound matters. Isolation is deadly. The land itself feels watchful. 
 
 Players will piece together a forgotten tragedy tied to the valley's rail and coal history. Survival depends on teamwork, courage, and knowing when not to follow the call.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Greenbrier Valley is wrapped in fog and old stories. Abandoned rail lines cut through dense forest, river mist rolls in against the wind, and locals know better than to travel alone after dark. When people begin vanishing along back roads and forgotten tunnels, the only clue left behind is a long, echoing whistling drifting through the trees. 
 
 This Monster of the Week one-shot is a slow-burn Appalachian horror mystery focused on investigation, atmosphere, and creeping dread. Expect missing persons, unreliable witnesses, and folklore passed down in half-whispered warnings. The thread doesn't chase--it lures. Sound matters. Isolation is deadly. The land itself feels watchful. 
 
 Players will piece together a forgotten tragedy tied to the valley's rail and coal history. Survival depends on teamwork, courage, and knowing when not to follow the call.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Great Alchemist's Tower Robbery
Short Description: Scale a crooked alchemist's tower to steal a volatile formula. Dodge traps, charm homunculi, and outwit rival thieves to be the first to the treasure, before everything blows up.
Long Description: Under the cover of a sickly green moon, the party infiltrates the tower spire of a reclusive alchemist whose experiments have grown dangerously unstable. The job: steal a legendary formula before it's sold off to the highest bidder. The tower is a vertical maze of alchemical traps, shifting rooms, magical security, and eccentric constructs that follow warped logic. Each floor presents new complications: volatile potions, living spells, and rival thieves. Planning, teamwork, and improvisation are key as alarms trigger, experiments escape containment, and the tower itself begins to react to the intrusion. The one-shot blends clever problem-solving, social manipulation, tense stealth, comical interactions, and explosive consequences.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Tower of Gaxx: Meatgrinder (for charity)
Short Description: Are you ready to strap on your sword & shield like it was 1977? Can you survive an old school dungeon designed as Gary Gygax intended: fast & furious with enemies & traps lurking behind every door?
Long Description: Join us as we play the game that started it all and remember the man who created an industry. The Meatgrinder is played arcade style with additional generics allowing you to re-spawn and continue your adventures, or new players to rotate in when you're had enough! New events start every hour. As in previous years, this event remains a charity event. Explore the Tower of the mighty Archmage Gaxx, whose awesome wizardly powers led to the rise of a great city in the nearby countryside. But Gaxx was seduced by an evil god, and the ensuing conflict led to the fall and destruction of both the city and his Tower. Come discover, explore, and delve into the deeper levels and chambers found beneath the Tower of Gaxx! What new treasures and artifacts can be found? What deadly traps and monsters lie ready for the unwary adventurer? Walk-ins welcome. Paper tickets accepted.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: Strong Female Character - A Discussion with Femme Creators on their Gaming Industry Experience
Short Description: Our panel of femme TTRPG creators & games industry veterans share honest accounts of their work experiences and offer advice for anyone looking to begin a similar professional journey.
Long Description:
DISCLAIMER! This is not a panel about writing for fiction or character concept art. There will be no list of required elements or sharing of a secret recipe which awards the elusive, and ineffable label in the title to fictional women.  
 This panel is about real femme creators and professionals working within the games industry. Our panelists will be sharing frank and open accounts of their experiences working within these professional and creative environments, to offer a picture of the current state of affairs for femme individuals looking to pursue a similar career/creator path. Come listen to our panelists who are achieving incredible accolades, working to create beautiful art and experiences for others, and all too often working twice as hard as what is fair for the recognition and respect they deserve.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Natalie Fuinha, Lua Stardust, Hannah Cadenazzi, Michele Robertson, Sachiko Morita
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

As in previous years, this event remains a charity event. Explore the Tower of the mighty Archmage Gaxx, whose awesome wizardly powers led to the rise of a great city in the nearby countryside. But Gaxx was seduced by an evil god, and the ensuing conflict led to the fall and destruction of both the city and his Tower. Come discover, explore, and delve into the deeper levels and chambers found beneath the Tower of Gaxx! What new treasures and artifacts can be found? What deadly traps and monsters lie ready for the unwary adventurer? Walk-ins welcome. Paper tickets accepted.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Railgun Road
Short Description: A gonzo, high-octane post-apocalyptic adventure set in a scorched wasteland. Civilization is gone, replaced by radioactive craters, mutant warbands, and blazing meteors that streak across the sky.
Long Description: A malformed motorcycle gang didn’t just steal supplies - they took a vital gasoline truck, a crate of rocket launchers, and the governor’s most beloved possession: Immortal Normal, his 33-year-old dog who refuses to die. The town answers by sending its toughest survivors, the PCs, into the desert to hunt down three feuding gang chiefs scattered across miles of hostile terrain.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Never Forget to Die
Short Description: A slick, cinematic 1960s spy thriller. In the shadows of the Cold War, British Intelligence uncovers a stolen Russian drug that could rewrite minds, shift power, and tip the balance of the world.
Long Description: The Soviets have sourced a powerful amnesia drug from a secretive inventor. After months of searching, MI:6 has discovered the middleman for the drug. Elite agents must parachute on to a luxury cruise ship to find the name of the inventor. From there, the agents are embroiled in a global race with the KGB to find the scientist and stop him from selling the drug… or persuade him he’s selling to the wrong side!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Roll a Con Save: A Workshop of Creator Fatigue & Burnout
Short Description: We've all experienced burnout and creative fatigue, so let's start the conversation on how to deal with it. Come chat with mental health professionals in the tabletop space about how to cope!
Long Description: Deadlines approaching? At the end of your rope with videos to make and no spoons to do it? Feeling the stress of freelance work? This workshop run by mental health professionals within the TTRPG space is aimed to help you build your resilience and fight the ultimate BBEG: burnout! Attendees will leave with tools help make change in their lives and keep their creative fires burning.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Wake of Blood (Play with a Designer)
Short Description: While the Revolutionary War rages, the party sails up the Mississippi River into the heart of darkness to investigate rumors of a rebel expedition gone rogue
Long Description: Face off against British-aligned frontier militias as you follow reports of burned settlements and massacres, all laid at the feet of one enigmatic, unaccountable American rebel. In this Nations & Cannons game, play as a 3rd-level, historically-sourced character from the American Revolution; complete with new rules for black powder firearms, period-appropriate Backgrounds, and brand-new classes and subclasses. Take on the redcoats and more!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Flintlock Fantasy: The Salem Reckoning (Play with a Designer)
Short Description: A century has passed since the Trials, but their legacy is not buried. Now, a Reckoning has come to Salem, with the players caught in the middle! Survive the past in this Revolutionary adventure!
Long Description: It has been over 80 years since the last accused witch was killed in Salem, Massachusetts, but the legacy of the infamous Trials is far from buried. Join an elite company of Continental soldiers as they uncover the deadly supernatural secrets behind the Salem Reckoning! In this Nations & Cannons game, you will play as a 4th-level, historically-sourced character from the American Revolution; complete with new rules for black powder firearms, period-appropriate Backgrounds, and brand-new classes and subclasses. Take on the Redcoats and more!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Whites of Their Eyes: The Battle of Bunker Hill (Play with a Designer)
Short Description: The British are coming! The ragtag militia in Boston is under threat as a fearsome force of redcoat grenadiers storms the Patriot lines! Can you repulse the enemy in this Revolutionary adventure?
Long Description:
Play with the Designer games come with your choice of a PDF of our core Rules or a 10 Dollar booth voucher.
 
 It’s summer, 1776. While the Patriots outside Boston have the British garrison under siege, most of the ragtag militia stationed on Bunker (and Breed’s) Hill has never fired a shot in anger. As the redcoats prepare a fearsome sortie, it falls to you to extract key intelligence before all hell breaks loose! In this Nations & Cannons game, you will play as a 4th-level, historically-sourced character from the American Revolution; complete with new rules for black powder firearms, period-appropriate Backgrounds, and brand-new classes and subclasses. Take on the Redcoats and more!

Title: A Reckoning for Daniel Boone (Play with the Designer)

Play with the Designer games come with your choice of a PDF of our core Rules or a 10 Dollar booth voucher.
 
 wo fathers try to avenge their sons, a daughter tries to save her father, and all events lead towards legendary American frontiersman Daniel Boone. Title: Flintlock Fantasy: Benjamin Franklin, Banshee Slayer (Play with the Designer)
Short Description: Join a spooktacular performance that you won’t soon forget! Glass armonicas, frightful spirits, and an iconic American Founder collide in this adventure!
Long Description:
Play with the Designer games come with your choice of a PDF of our core Rules or a 10 Dollar booth voucher.
 
 When a band of Continental operatives look for some R&R in Philadelphia, they find themselves attending a glass armonica performance by Marianne Davis. But not all is as it seems, as they find themselves confronting a deadly spectral foe. Coming to their aid is none other than Poor Richard himself, Benjamin Franklin! Take on the Redcoats and more in this Revolutionary 5E setting!

Title: Earth Crawl: Scrap Metal
Short Description: You are convicted of a crime and sent to the surface of the post-apocalyptic Earth to recover material vital to the survival of the colonies. But will you survive? Playtest a new setting from BVP!
Long Description:
After the fall of Earth, people hang on by a thread, surviving in orbital colonies and eeking out a living while the megacorps and few remaining governments control the moon and the mega-colonies. Highly educated, deeply trained, you still sacrifice everything to pay for oxygen, food, and radiation-reduced living space. Your crushing debt has led to you being convicted of vagrancy or some other crime, and you are sentenced to the surface to recover materials the colonies need to survive.
 
 This is a playtest of a funnel for a new DCC/MCC mash-up setting from Blind Visionary Publications. Players will get to experience the adventure and provide feedback for improving it as we move towards publication. All players will leave the game with a copy of an Earth Crawl Ashcan 2.0 class!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Running RPGs for Strangers & New Players
Short Description: Introducing new players to your game? New games to old players? Wanna run something for the fine folks at Gen Con, but not sure if you’re ready? We’ve got you.
Long Description: The hive mind at Wizard Staffing put together our guide to crafting the right atmosphere for newcomers. Our battle-tested tactics can help whether you’re diving into a new system with your crew, or hosting a 5e session at your local game store. We’ll start small with player-friendly rules. Get the players comfortable. Then establish character relationships. Quickly. It’s equal parts storytelling, persuasion, and smooth facilitation. Whether you’re a seasoned GM or just stepping into the role, you’ll walk away with tips, tricks, and tales to run sessions that are both inclusive and leave players wanting more. We all want to welcome fresh faces to the table. Connect with fellow game masters and let’s make it easier.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Pict of the Crop
Short Description: Britannia 229CE. A Brigantes village has reported an unusually poor harvest, followed by unexplained disappearances. The Brotherhood of Mithras are sent to investigate.
Long Description:
In 229CE, Hadrian's Wall in Britannia, and the brave auxiliary soldiers who man it, provide the physical security for the northern frontier of the mighty Roman Empire. However, only an enlightened few understand the graver supernatural threats lurking amid the dark forests and trackless marshes of the border region - and in the hearts of men. Those enlightened few are yourselves, the Brotherhood of Mithras, who serve among the auxiliaries. 
 The Prefect of your cohort has another mission for you, though thankfully this one seems less politically fraught than the last couple. Villagers from the Brigantes tribe south of the fort have reported an unusually poor harvest, followed by strange disappearances - and for some reason the Prefect seems to care enough to send you to investigate.
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Truth is Cooler than Fiction: Using Your Community's Hidden History as Inspiration for Your Campaign
Short Description: Make your campaign unforgettable using resources in your own backyard - the history, stories, and legacy of your own community!
Long Description: This workshop will highlight how to find and utilize the quirky history, unsung heroes, and spooky spots of your own hometown to create characters, adventures, and settings that will thrill your players. We’ll talk about utilizing physical structures and landmarks so your players will be reminded of your adventures as they pass that haunted water tower. You also have the chance to lift up incredible local figures who’s stories might not be as well known. The workshop focuses on how to do this responsibly, with an eye to centering the stories of marginalized folks.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Escape from Arnon's Keep
Short Description: Learn to create characters and play this new game with a special introductory adventure!
Long Description: Many adventurers have heard stories of untold riches stashed away in the mad wizard Arnon’s mysterious keep nestled somewhere deep in the Valris Tops. Some die trying to find it, withering under the scorching desert sun and lost in the sea of identical mountain peaks. Other adventurers - like yourselves - actually made it through the front door to behold an unmatched trove filled with treasures of indescribable form, function, and value. Any single item from this hoard could fund a lifetime of heroic deeds. However, as is so often the case, things may seem a little too good to be true. When the heavy iron door slams shut behind you on the way in and sparks with magic, sealing you inside, you find yourselves with a formidable challenge: to find a way out!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: Horse Eater
Short Description: Many dangers surround the small town of Deadwood, but when a group of Miners are found dead, questions arise. Who were the real murderers and what's got everyone so spooked?
Long Description: Both seasoned and new players alike will experience the core components that make Deadlands tick. Deadwood can be a dangerous place but something isn't right about the recent murders just out of town. Made to look like they were done by local Sioux but there's one too many details that don't add up. In turn, a group of Sioux invoke the aid of your Posse to help get to the bottom of the matter to prevent further bloodshed. The further the Posse ventures, the weirder things begin to get. You will have to use quick thinking and even quicker reflexes to survive The Horse Eater.

Title: Authentic Creature Characters Workshop
Short Description: This creative workshop is designed for role-players, home-brewers, & creators who enjoy fantastical realms & alien worlds. It would encourage attendees to participate via writing & acting exercises.
Long Description: My name is Brent Winzek; I’m an English professor, science fiction author, & owner of Space Cadets Studios (an award-winning independent publishing & multimedia company). Through my writing program, I’ve developed a variety of techniques (based on acting & writing exercises) that workshop observations about body structure and physicality, as well as ways to take very human critiques of society and inject them into a different creature’s perspective. I employ a variety of playful acting & writing exercises to encourage otherworldly thinking. The goal is to encourage other creatives to think deeper about character voices from any imagined origin.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Materials Required Details: Paper & pen or pencil

Group: Space Cadets Studios | Author & Educator Brent Winzek
Title: Build a Better Bard
Short Description: The aim of this event is to invite role-players, home-brewers, & creators to practice some bard skills by understanding & performing Shakespeare in a safe, playful space.
Long Description: English is a complicated language! Early Modern English - that's the language of Shakespeare - is even harder. That's probably why most people toss in a little 'thy' and a dash of 'thou' and think they're good. But that couldn't be more inaccurate! My name is Brent Winzek; I’m an English professor, science fiction author, & owner of Space Cadets Studios (an award-winning indie publishing & multimedia company). A niche expertise of mine in theater and performance is understanding, translating, and performing the Early Modern English of Shakespeare. As with our ‘Modern English,’ Early Modern English has many nuances and quirks to master. This workshop builds a basic grasp of easy terms & phrases from my dramaturgy work with undergraduate performers. Attendees partake in acting exercises to engage with the language.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Historicity of King Arthur: One Thousand Years of Academic Slap-Fights
Short Description: King Arthur is one of the most famous historical figures in British history… isn’t he? Come and learn all the evidence for and against and decide for yourself!
Long Description: King Arthur is one of the most famous historical figures in British history… isn’t he? In fact, ever since his introduction in the early Middle Ages, Arthur has been caught in a whirlpool of controversy. He’s had medieval haters and staunch defenders and lots of British monarchs who happily used his story for propaganda. But who was right? Has either side struck a killing blow? Come and learn all the evidence for and against and decide for yourself!

Come in, select an adorable bear, and then we walk you step by step and teach you how to make a vest for your very own bear. As always LOANER tools are provided and we accept overflow based on space limitations but try to accommodate all we can. We are honored and humbled at how popular this class has become, so while we can't take everyone at once, we do our best to help as many as possible. If we're sold out and you wish to come, send a message and we'll be able to give you updated information as it's available.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Learn to Make Chainmaille: Bracelet or Other Projects
Short Description: MM's small-sized chainmaille buffet! Here, you can pick 1 of 4 kits: encapsulated marble keychain, or a chainmaille diamond, or a Byzantine-variant bracelet or a wrapped D20.
Long Description: In this class, you pick one of four items to make, and then we teach you how to do it. The projects available for this set are: first an encapsulated marble, where we teach you to make an individual bag of holding for a marble, we provide, that makes a great key fob! Our next option is a D20 chainmaille keychain where you wrap a D20 with rings. The next is a diamond shaped key fob made from chainmaille rings. The last option for this time block is a bracelet made from a variant of the Byzantine weave. Come, pick which project you would like to make, and we walk you through it step by step. As always loaner tools are provided and we accept overflow based on space limitations but try to accommodate all we can. If we're sold out and you wish to come, send an email and we'll be able to give you updated information as it's available.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Gimme Da Diversity: Adding More Multicultural Influences to TTRPG's
Short Description: Want your fantasy world to be as diverse as the players at your table? Want to add influences from other cultures without being appropriative? The Gimme Da Loot crew is here for you!
Long Description: The cast of Gimme Da Loot will coach players and game masters how to move beyond traditional fantasy tropes to incorporate diverse perspectives into class builds, homebrew creations, and storytelling themes. Whether it's LL Cool J Barbarians or the Aswang, the GMDL cast will give you the tools you need to make the next generation of games contain content as diverse as the players. Event will include a discussion of incorporating influences without being appropriative, a brainstorming session for a homebrew class or setting based on suggestions from attendees, and close out with a Q&A Session
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Critical Femininity: Women & Femme Voices in TTRPGs
Short Description: Join a panel of femme-identifying D&D players as they explore the intersections of femininity and the TTRPG's.
Long Description: Join a panel of femme-identifying D&D players as they explore the intersections of femininity and the TTRPG space. Through personal stories and discussion, they’ll share how their experiences with femininity have shaped their gameplay, roleplaying choices, and interactions within the community—both positively and negatively. From character creation to navigating tabletop culture, this conversation will delve into the challenges, joys, and nuances of bringing femme perspectives to the table. Guiding the discussion is Gimme Da Loot’s favorite non-binary ooze, Mori—voiced by Tori—who will help foster an open, engaging, and insightful conversation about what it means to embrace femininity in the world of TTRPGs.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The School of Movie Magic
Short Description: How do movies influence TTRPG's? How can you take the films you love and turn them into fresh content for your table? An actor, screenwriter, and film podcaster discuss adaptations and systems.
Long Description: From systems based on movies to how movies can inspire fresh content for game masters, 3 dungeon masters do a deep dive into the film influences on the greatest game. Join veteran actor/stuntman Christian Stokes (Five Nights at Freddies), screenwriter Wyndll Robertson (Prison Break), and TTRPG podcaster Turner C (Save Vs Cinema) for a discussion and Q&A session.
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)

Title: Castle Ravenloft
Short Description: Your party is summoned into a gothic nightmare, where shifting secrets, undead horrors, and ancient terror await.
Long Description:
Come play original Advanced Dungeons and Dragons in one of the original gothic horror settings of Ravenloft. Players will be provided premade characters. Adventure slightly condensed to ensure enough time to complete.

Title: Gothic Horror in Ravenloft
Short Description: A cursed manor sits vacant, its halls echoing with the whispers of the dead. To break the curse, the heroes must face the house’s horrors and uncover the family’s buried secret.
Long Description:
Experience a classic gothic horror adventure set in world of Ravenloft and 1st Edition Advanced Dungeons and Dragons. 
 
 Whispers seep from Gryphon Hill Manor, long abandoned after its guests vanished in a single night. Now the Hollow Guest—an echoing mysterious figure —stalks its long silent halls. As the manor shifts and memories come alive, heroes must uncover the truth before they, too, become hollow.
 
 Rules will be taught and premade characters will be provided.

Title: The Tower of Gaxx: Special Event (for charity)
Short Description: Are you ready to strap on your sword & shield like it was 1976? This 4 hour event features one of our veteran DMs and has the party going through a module from his vast collection.
Long Description:
Are you itching to try your hand at surviving an old school pre-1978 D&D module? Are you good enough to solve the puzzles before time runs out? Can you work with your fellow adventurers and outsmart the monsters?
 
 Veteran DM Mike Boehm hosts a longer old school adventure. He has amassed a vast collection of modules from the early years of D&D. This is a limited engagement with five separate games scheduled; two each on Thursday, and Friday and one on Saturday. 
 
 Join us as we play the game that started it all and remember the man who created an industry. 
 As in previous years, this premium event remains a charity event. This is NOT a tournament.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Tower of Gaxx: Tomb It May Concern Event (for charity)
Short Description: Dare to enter the Tomb of Acererak, many have entered, none have returned! This Tomb of Horrors has been the demise of many an adventurer. Enter, and meet your worst nightmare!
Long Description:
Are you itching to try your hand at surviving one of the more challenging modules created by Gary Gygax? First revealed to the public at Origins I in 1975, Gary designed this adventure to test and challenge expert players. The Tomb of Horrors introduced to players the value of lugging around the ubiquitous 10 foot pole. It also opened their eyes to how deadly traps and puzzles can (and should) be.
 
 Veteran DM Stewart “the man of many voices” Beach hosts. This is a limited engagement with two separate games scheduled; one on Thursday and one on Saturday. 
 
 Join us as we play the game that started it all and remember the man who created an industry. 
 As in previous years, this premium event remains a charity event. This is NOT a tournament.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Tower of Gaxx: Retro Event (for charity)
Short Description: Lost for years in a box in a basement, a vague memory led to a dedicated hunt to find this treasure from 1976! This 4 hour event features one of our taller DMs and has the party questing for an item.
Long Description:
Looking to play something more retro than 1st Edition AD&D? Relive the days of original (not Advanced) D&D with a run-through of an Official GenCon IX Tournament Dungeon (August 1976) tweaked to allow 8 players to participate. Veteran DM Jesse Anderson hosts this longer old school adventure. Prior experience with older versions of D&D is recommended. 
 
 Premise: Your party has offended a great and powerful (and slightly confused) wizard, and he has asked you to retrieve a wand -or was it a staff- that was borrowed (or maybe stolen) from him long ago. 
 
 This is a limited engagement with two separate games scheduled; one on Friday and one on Saturday. 
 
 Join us as we play the game that started it all and remember the man who created an industry. 
 As in previous years, this premium event remains a charity event. This is NOT a tournament.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: So you want to be a (better) GM...
Short Description: Want to be a better GM? Looking to switch from player to GM? Then look no further – we have just the thing! This workshop will uncover system independent lessons learned the hard way!
Long Description: Remember the GMs who transformed simple RPG sessions into epic adventures that left us craving more? What about the GMs whose names we'd rather forget, who left us lamenting the time lost on lackluster misadventures? Is there a secret to becoming the legendary GM and not the forgettable one? Join Patrick Fait, a seasoned GM from the Tower of Gaxx, as he unveils the not-so-secret "secrets" to mastering the art of GMing that 45+ years of running RPGs has revealed to him. Topics include:Techniques to Engage and Captivate Your Players; Preparation, Flexibility and Adaptation; and Using Game Mechanics to Support Storytelling. But wait, THERE'S MORE! Besides acquiring knowledge, there will be a hands-on element to practice firsthand the principles discussed. No matter your preferred system, in-person or online, the guidance provided will enhance your game. This is a repeat of last year.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Storming the Poplar Palisades: A Mossflower Tale
Short Description: The evil wildcat Tsarmina has built a fort in the heart of Mossflower Forest. Can you destroy the fort and give Martin the Warrior enough time to return from Salamandastron?
Long Description:
The vile Tsarmina Greeneyes has built a new fort in the heart of Mossflower Wood, intent on crushing the woodland rebellion from outside the walls of Kotir. 
 
 With Dinny, Martin, and Gonff searching for the Lord of fabled Salamandastron, the Council of Resistance in Mossflower has asked its bravest warriors to destroy the fort to allow enough time to flood the halls of Kotir. Can you rise to the challenge?
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: On Matters Best Left Buried
Short Description: In the cold lands of Skyrim, villagers have asked the College of Winterhold to investigate a child's claim that her dead father has returned, while many in the village insist it is nothing.
Long Description:
To the Esteemed Masters of the College,
 I write at the urging of several concerned townsfolk regarding a matter we are ill-equipped to resolve. A local child, Ingrid, daughter of the late Hrolf, has been claiming with increasing insistence that she sees her father standing outside her window at night. The man was buried a month past, and no one has seen anything amiss.
 Most believe the girl is simply struggling with her grief. Still, her stories have become persistent, detailed, and disruptive. She refuses to sleep, speaks of footsteps where none are found, and has begun frightening other children. It is unsettling the village, and we would prefer the matter settled.
 If this is nothing, we ask that you put the child’s fears to rest. If it is something more, we believe it wiser that the College investigate before rumor and superstition take hold.
 —Signed, on behalf of the village
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Dungeons & Dragons Lego Movie
Short Description: Everything is still awesome! Play through the Dungeons and Dragons Lego adventure “Red Dragon’s Tale” as Emmet, Wyldstyle, Unikitty, and the rest!
Long Description:
For 29 years I’ve run silly games at Gen Con by taking two unlike genres and slamming them together to see what dumb stuff comes out. It’s time for one final rerun of this adventure before this Lego set retires forever!
 
 Get ready to explore the world of “Red Dragon’s Tale” as the cast of “The Lego Movie” on a fully built Lego adventure map! Use your creativity and skills as a master builder to create items to use in game! Can you save the realm and discover who is behind a nefarious plot to conquer reality? Or at least earn some delicious hot wings? Don’t worry if you don’t know the difference between Ninjago and Thac0, you’ll be laughing by the end!
 
 Rules light and fun heavy!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Canceled Crossover Crisis
Short Description: Your show was canceled! Your group of studio rejects have decided to infiltrate and take over another popular show! Can you reclaim your role as a star or will you end up on the cutting room floor?
Long Description:
For 29 years I’ve run silly games at Gen Con by taking two unlike genres and slamming them together to see what silly stuff comes out. Well, this year, we’re getting meta, because we’re not only mashing up genres, but game systems as well!
 
 For the first time ever, Toon is collaborating with Advanced Dimensional Green Ninja-Educational Preparatory Super-Elementary Fortress 555 from Windmill Games! In this insane crossover, you’ll be playing as a group of cartoons whose show just got canceled! The only way to survive is to infiltrate another show. You’ll be going up against another table of gamers playing as the cast of ADGNEPSEF555 while you and your team try to devise a way to become the new stars! Can you reclaim your former glory, or will you be a flop?
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Making Room at the Table: Inclusive Worldbuiding
Short Description: When creating a world or an adventure, do you invite everyone in, or, without realizing it, are there people you are excluding? Learn about implicit bias and ways to create more inclusive worlds.
Long Description:
Implicit bias affects our understanding, actions, and decisions without conscious awareness. Everyone exhibits it to a greater or lesser extent, and it is an inherent part of being human.
 
 Recognizing that, what can you do to make your adventures and worlds more inclusive and inviting?
 
 The first steps are to learn – what is your perspective, who are you excluding, and what is their viewpoint (and their own biases). While this takes a lifetime of practice, you can start in an hour, learning about implicit biases and ways to overcome them, exploring how the design of space can be inviting or disinviting, and coming away with some tools to further explore after the con.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Haunted Walking Tour of Indy
Short Description: Join us for a tour of darkness which visits many reportedly haunted sites of Indy. You'll hear chilling tales & macabre history.
Long Description: We'll visit places of ghastly phantoms with hopes of capturing a glimpse, for ourselves, of specters from beyond the veil. Join us for a 90-minute unscripted tour of darkness with “Master of the Macabre” Mike McDowell and Chris McDowell of Chaos Haunted Tours and the Indiana Ghost Trackers. We’ll take a step into the notoriously haunted downtown district and visit places of ghostly encounters with hopes of capturing a glimpse, for ourselves, of these specters from beyond the veil. During the tour, you’ll hear frightening tales, Stories of hienous murders and some of the rich history of the city. We’ll discuss actual evidence that we’ve found while ghost hunting in Indianapolis and various other Indiana locations. Please note that this tour does NOT go inside any of the stops.

Short Description: Join us for a tour of darkness which visits many reportedly haunted sites of Indy. You'll hear chilling tales & macabre history.
Long Description: We'll visit places of ghastly phantoms with hopes of capturing a glimpse, for ourselves, of specters from beyond the veil. Join us for a 90-minute unscripted tour of darkness with “Master of the Macabre” Mike McDowell and Chris McDowell of Chaos Haunted Tours and the Indiana Ghost Trackers. We’ll take a step into the notoriously haunted downtown district and visit places of ghostly encounters with hopes of capturing a glimpse, for ourselves, of these specters from beyond the veil. During the tour, you’ll hear frightening tales, Stories of hienous murders and some of the rich history of the city. We’ll discuss actual evidence that we’ve found while ghost hunting in Indianapolis and various other Indiana locations. Please note that this tour does NOT go inside any of the stops.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: The Drowned Knight
Short Description: You are aspiring knights, venturing into the wild reaches of the realm on a quest to prove yourselves worthy. What wrongs will you right and lessons will you learn along the way?
Long Description:
A PbtA game about knightly quests and coming of age, inspired by Arthurian legends, fairy tales, Tamora Pierce's Tortall books, and Magic: The Gathering's plane of Eldraine. The game is in-development from Cloven Pine Games. It uses modular playbooks where each character is created by combining two playbooks that form two halves of a heraldic shield, e.g. The Stag and The Anvil, or The Fox and The Oak.
 
 In this scenario, the Knight-Aspirants are called upon to protect a seaside town from a terrifying apparition—The Drowned Knight. Why has he been dragging villagers off into the mist, and how will he strike next?

Title: Sage's Sabbatical: Escape from the Arcanist's Horn

Spellcasters are considered to have any spell component they need to cast their prepared spells. The adventure will take place in a single adventuring day with no preemptive abilities used the day before. Pre-generated characters will be available if you do not bring your own.

Title: The Safe Table
Short Description: Need fresh ideas to support players at your table? Seek no further, grab a seat, and be regaled with the tales of our experiences creating connection at our tables as mental health professionals.

Are you wondering how safety and community can improve your games? Would you like to learn more about how to foster a safe environment at different types of tables? Are you interested in learning more about how TTRPGs can be used therapeutically? If so, join Vanessa Davis, LPC/MHSP-S, Kiela L. Hinson, LPC/MHSP, and Zach Stenzel LPC/MHSP as they facilitate an in-depth discussion on these very topics!
 
 Prepare to be wow-ed by our canva skills, bad puns, and open and honest discussion about why safety in TTRPGs matters. We will explore multiculturalism in TTRPGs, safety tools and resources that you may use at your very own table, explore why simple math is so challenging for therapists, all followed by a q&a.

Title: Dice Testing Probabilities
Short Description: We have doing the chi-square test wrong. Come get the actual 95% values of chi-square statistic that you should be using.
Long Description: The chi-square statistic is only asymptotically chi-square distributed. The exact 95% values of the statistic are a function of the number of dice test rolls. The values approach the chi-square distribution values only slowly (1/N). This seminar will provide the exact 95% values of the statistic for up to 3000 rolls.

Title: Summon Achilles' Wrath: Turn Public Domain Legends (or Your Own Characters) into Living AI NPCs

Long Description:

 Dr. Jo Szewczyk (Surviving Gen X; forthcoming Scarlet Shadows), PhD Creative Writing, MSc Oxford, Vanderbilt GenAI, shows you how with ChatGPT. Bonus: deep-dive history/books via authentic AI chats.
 
 Free tier works (rate limits possible at con—build before/after). Paid smoother. Templates work on Grok/Claude.
 
 Core Focus: Build one dynamic NPC from public domain classics or your writing. Master few-shot, guardrails, flipped interactions for true voice, memory, grudges.
 
 Hands-On: Bring phone/laptop. Craft live, probe player-style chats—Achilles rages, Holmes unravels lies. Leave with reusable templates.
 
 GMs, writers, LARPers—no AI experience needed. Legal, fast, deepens games + knowledge.

Title: Kink 101
Short Description: Curious about kink? What does BDSM really mean? How can you do it safely by yourself or with partner(s)? Start your kinky journey with a good foundation here by covering introductory kink topics.
Long Description:
Kink is all around us in society but how do you engage in it? Kink isn’t all dark dungeons and dangerous situations. Kink can be serious or silly, dark or colorful, heavy or light, but most importantly…fun! Kink is customizable and with this class, you’ll have the knowledge to start having your own fun in a risk aware and responsible way.
 
 In this class, taught by two experienced lifestyle kinksters who have given presentations across the country, we will cover consent and safety, what kink is and isn’t, how to negotiate play, and some common terms used in the lifestyle. The goal of this class is to give you the foundation you need to start exploring with yourself or with partner(s) in a risk aware way. There will be no play in this class and questions will be answered if time permits.

Title: Maps: Worldbuilding for Lore & Gameplay
Short Description: Traditional fantasy cartographers, designers, and encounter map artists explore how maps shape worlds, conflict, strategy, lore, and player decisions to create epic play.
Long Description: Whether enriching a battle or encounter, revealing the conflicts of a campaign, or deepening the story of a setting, maps are more than navigation tools—they are narrative devices. This panel brings together fantasy mapmakers and artists from across the TTRPG space—game creators, battle and encounter map designers, and traditional fantasy cartographers—to discuss how maps function as worldbuilding tools for both lore and gameplay. Maps actively shape player decisions, reinforce pacing, and drive story, rather than simply depicting space. Learn how battle map design can drive conflict and shape encounters, and how world maps communicate politics, power, and struggles for resources. Panelists will share techniques and insights for creating maps that do more than guide—they enrich the adventure, blending strategy, narrative, and creativity into unforgettable play experiences.

Title: No More Side Quests: Brain-Friendly Planning for Puppeteers & Creatives
Short Description: Level up with tools & strategies that work *with* your brain, not against it. You'll pick a project & leave with a customized roadmap & tools that turn your creative ideas into finished main quests!
Long Description:
Stacey Gordon is no stranger to missed deadlines or ideas that never left the page (if they even made it there in the first place). But she's also picked up & figured out strategies that have helped her work with the brain she was given, not the one her teachers "saw potential" in. She's figured out how to use her neurodivergence for good (never evil) (ok, maybe a little bit of evil), and live a life with less stress and less burn-out. 
 
 Stacey has been coaching other creatives like you to achieve their creative goals for over half a decade. Together they've been able to celebrate hundreds of accomplished goals! Come learn the strategies to hone in on your priorities, stay focused, and be inspired to level up, finish those quests and be proud of yourself!

Title: Upgrade Your FDM Prints
Short Description: Upgrade basic FDM prints with fast surface treatments. Learn sanding, fillers, carving, and texture tricks to hide layer lines and create believable stone, wood, and metal finishes.
Long Description:
Learn how to turn basic FDM prints into convincing tabletop terrain using fast, practical surface treatments. This hands-on workshop covers sanding, fillers, carving, and texture techniques that hide layer lines while creating believable stone, wood, metal, and concrete finishes that read clearly at gaming distance.
 
 Participants will work on sample prints while learning how to choose the right treatment for different materials, add scale-appropriate detail, and avoid common mistakes that waste time or muddy forms. The focus is on repeatable methods you can use on terrain, scatter pieces, and props printed at home—no advanced painting skills or specialized tools required.
 
 Perfect for terrain builders, RPG dungeon masters, and hobbyists who want better-looking results from everyday printers.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Basing & Composition
Short Description: Improve miniature basing and diorama composition using simple materials and clear design rules. Learn layout, scale, and storytelling techniques to make your minis read at tabletop distance.
Long Description:
Learn practical basing and composition techniques that make miniatures read clearly on the gaming table using simple materials and strong design principles. This hands-on workshop focuses on layout, scale, focal points, and visual storytelling so your minis feel intentional and cohesive without requiring advanced painting skills.
 
 Participants will build sample bases and arrange small composition setups while learning how to match bases to terrain, create believable environments, and avoid common mistakes like cluttered layouts or inconsistent scale. The emphasis is on fast, repeatable methods you can apply across an army or RPG collection.
 
 Perfect for wargamers, hobbyists, and terrain builders who want their miniatures to feel grounded in a world. Attendees receive an informational handout with composition tips and basing recipes for future projects.

Long Description: In this class, students will learn the True Metallic Metal technique, using metallic paint and matte paint to create shadows and highlights for a more realistic metal finish. Title: Whispers from the Deep - Midnight Sun
Short Description: Devil’s Gulch is silent. Team up with 6 kobolds to uncover the mystery, grab the Mind Shard, and escape “alive” in this high-octane magipunk heist.
Long Description:
Special Horror Edition! 
 
 Kobold Quest: Dust & Devils is a high-octane, improv-driven, rules-light, card-powered narrative heist set in a lawless western steampunk magipunk frontier. Devil’s Gulch has gone dark. Travelers vanish. The wildlife has fled. Vultures circle above an empty town that should not be empty.
 
 Gather up to six other kobolds and venture into the silent streets to uncover the truth, seize the mysterious Mind Shard, and escape… “alive.” This two-hour, loosely cooperative chaos-fest rewards bold creativity, reckless teamwork, and increasingly catastrophic decisions as you break in, grab the prize, and get out — preferably before everything (and everyone) explodes.
 
 What is the Mind Shard? Why do you need it? How many kobolds can you fit in a trench coat? Only time will tell.
 
 Presented by Kobold Game Labs — please use your Kobold Posse Responsibly.

Title: The Ember Event
Short Description: Trapped in a deadly time loop, you and up to 6 kobolds must unravel the Time Shard’s mystery and stop an arcane cataclysm in this fast-paced, chaotic magipunk adventure.
Long Description:
You awaken with a box. A single glowing rune burns across its surface. You step into town - moments later, the sky tears open in an arcane cataclysm. Everyone dies. You awaken again. The box remains. Now two runes glow.
 
 Kobold Quest: Dust & Devils is a high-octane, improv-driven, rules-light, card-powered narrative adventure set in a lawless western steampunk magipunk frontier. Trapped in a deadly time loop, you and up to six other kobolds must explore the town, unravel the mystery of the Time Shard, and break the cycle before the world ends - again.
 
 This two-hour, loosely cooperative chaos-fest rewards bold creativity, reckless teamwork, and increasingly catastrophic decisions as you experiment, adapt, and desperately try to escape… “alive.”
 
 What is the Time Shard? Why is it bound to you? How many kobolds can you fit in a trench coat?
 
 Only time will tell.
 Track 3 (2026)

Title: John Company Megagame
Short Description: Team up with a family to co-run a single player position in this megagame version of the award-winning Wehrlegig history game.
Long Description:
"No empire springs into being fully-formed. Their genealogies are as messy as their modes of operation." - Cole Wehrle.
 History is so often presented in broad strokes. The battles, the wars, the treaties, the laws, the deals, the crimes. Names get attached to these events, known as the movers of history. The Hastings’, the Larkins’, the Paxton’s. Behind these names are a multitude of interwoven stories that become the true fabric of history. It often takes digging through the archives to understand just how important those stories are. John Company Second Edition from Wehrlegig Games, is one of the most poignant reflections on the means and motivations of colonial expansion in the 18th and 19th Century using the family as a lens. With The Company Archives, players dig deeper into the game’s argument, through an RPG experience layered on top of the boardgame play.
