Title: Designing Serious Games
Short Description: Back for another year, this updated combo seminar-workshop introduces key design features of serious games and then guides audience members through the design process with a custom scenario.
Long Description:
It is becoming more common to use games in the classroom - and for good reason! Research shows that games can help motivate and engage people to learn and try new things. But what about outside of the classroom; can we use games to teach skills, to promote health, or to change hearts and minds? This is where serious games come in: games that are entertaining, but have a purpose beyond just entertainment. 
 
 The event will begin with an introduction to designing serious games. Then, in small groups, audience members will use what they learned to address a scenario by planning and designing a game which incorporates key design features of serious games. 
 
 All levels of experience are welcome! Whether you already have an idea for your own serious games, or you just want to learn more about this very interesting design process, this event is for you.

The old king is gone, and the throne has claim unclaimed for too long.
 Play as an heir to a crumbling kingdom, as you vie for control of vital regions and political councils. Immerse yourself in a richly illustrated world of strategy and tactical gambits, where every card is a window into a fantastical land.
 Govern, journey and clash with your rivals at the head of an asymmetric faction, as you work to bolster you bid with new allies, artefacts and positions of power. 
 
 Wits, wagers, royalty and ruses.
 
 What crown will you wear?
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: No, this event does not require tickets!

Title: Arcs
Short Description: A sharp sci-fi strategy game, set in a dark yet silly universe. Take actions with multi-use cards and define the objectives that everyone competes over.

Title: Crazy Underpainting to Make Your Miniatures Pop
Short Description: Let's explore how powerful colors can be the perfect trick to start off your miniature with a BANG! Speed painting your D&D character? Looking to add more wow factor to your models? Lets begin!
Long Description:
In this class, we explore the use of powerful colors as 'underpainting' to start our miniature off with drama and a colorful POP. This technique is usable regardless of your painting level, and once we learn the basics we will show how to finish your model quickly with a few more tricks that lean into embracing the drama that only color can bring! 
 
 If you sign up for this course, you will be sent an email with the few tools you will want to bring to the class (brushes, model holder, etc.) AND the ability to email me any questions you may have before the class!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: Cinder & Ash - Midnight Sun
Short Description: Chaotic kobolds race a dragon and a devil-bound tyrant to steal an ancient relic. Cut deals, spark mayhem, and betray everyone before the town burns to ash.
Long Description:
Special Horror Edition!
 
 Kobold Quest: Dust & Devils rides again in a blaze of magipunk mayhem! A dying frontier town hides an ancient relic humming with forbidden power—and every monster with ambition can smell it. Cinder, a fire-blooded dragon, wants it to rule the wastes. Lord Ashcroft, a silver-tongued tyrant, plans to crack it open and ascend as an archfiend. And your band of reckless kobolds? You’re here to steal it first.
 
 Roam dusty streets, spin rumors into leverage, duel rival gangs, rig contraptions, and cut deals that will absolutely backfire. Side with dragonfire or deviltry… or play them both. The cards drive the chaos, the story bends to your choices, and the clock is ticking. In Dust & Devils, loyalty is optional, explosions are inevitable, and tiny scaly legends are born in smoke and fire.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Raid on Castle Raisinloaf
Short Description: Homeless kobolds raid Castle Raisinloaf to seize Vampire Lord C'hahd's hidden Heart. Win a new home. Fail and become dinner. Chaos, guns, and magipunk mayhem!
Long Description:
In Kobold Quest: Dust & Devils, a narrative-driven, wild west, steampunk magipunk adventure bursting with chaos, light rules, and ridiculous fun, your homeless, hungry kobold clan is one bad torch away from extinction—and desperate times call for reckless real estate acquisition. The plan? Seize Castle Raisinloaf and make it yours.
 
 Unfortunately, it’s occupied by the brooding (and extremely bitey) Vampire Lord C'hahd and a buffet of monsters, mercenaries, and things that skitter in the dark. Rumor whispers his still-beating Heart lies hidden deep within the castle’s cursed halls. Capture it, and the pale tyrant must kneel. Fail, and you’re appetizers. Grab your six-shooters, your posse, and your questionable courage—tonight, the kobolds raid.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Riding the Wave: the Ups & Downs of a Career in Creativity
Short Description: Jennifer Barnhart takes an honest look at balancing artistic risk & personal comfort. Be inspired by how she chose when to leap or hold back, & how those choices helped her build a sustainable career.
Long Description:
As creatives, it's hard to know when to take a risk and when to protect your stability. Jennifer Barnhart will share stories from her career navigating creative risks, having steady work, needing this crazy little thing called "healthcare," and how to pursue a creatively satisfying life without sacrificing the necessities of life, or your own comfort.
 Jennifer's career spans many genres, including regional theater, puppetry, television, film, commercials, and voice over work. She deeply understands the ins & outs behind sustaining a long-term career as an artist. Let Jennifer inspire you with her insight on when to take a leap of faith, when to play it safe, and how to find the middle ground that puts your life in the Goldilocks Zone.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: To Introduce Our Guest Puppeteer...
Short Description: ...that's what we'd like to do. Emmy-nominated puppeteer, Jennifer Barnhart has brought some of your favorite characters to life on stage and screen & you just need to read the longer description!
Long Description: As we were saying... Jennifer Barnhart is a force of nature when it comes to not only a long-lasting career on stage & television, but also when it comes to being a wonderful human being. You'll know her best from television shows like Sesame Street (Gladys the Cow, Zoe, Maggie Cadabby, and Granny Bird, among others), Between the Lions (Cleo), Julie's Greenroom, Johnny and the Sprites, & Bear in the Big Blue House. Or you might know her from the original cast of the Tony Award-winning musical, Avenue Q (Mrs. Thistletwat & Bad Idea Bear)... Or from her regional theater roles like Lady Macbeth, Goneril, and Mrs. Cratchitt (The Arden, Goodspeed, Alabama Shakespeare Festival). But... she's also lent her talents to SNL, Last Week Tonight with John Oliver, The Pause with Sam Jay, and House of Cards, and Law & Order, SVU and SO MANY MORE THINGS! For real, you just need to come & hear about it!

Title: Just Lip Sync (For Puppetry)
Short Description: Learn how to move your puppet's mouth to make puppeteering easier, believable, and more engaging! Learn with Sesame Street performers, Jennifer Barnhart & Stacey Gordon, and have loads of fun!
Long Description: Moving your puppet's mouth at the right time & in the right way is a genuinely difficult skill to learn. The technique tends to function the opposite of how our brains learn, which can leave newer puppeteers wondering why their puppets aren’t performing at the same level as what they see on screen. Ideal for neurodivergent learners, this workshop is dedicated solely to refining that technique & gaining the tools to make it easier for your brain to make that connection & the skill easier to pick up. You’ll learn tips & tricks from experts in their field to work with your body instead of against it & have fun at the same time! You’re getting two, two, two instructors in one workshop! With a collective 44 years teaching puppetry, you can count on Jennifer & Stacey to teach you all of the secrets! Can it truly, actually, really be true that relaxation is the key to success? Come find out!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Raid on Castle Raisinloaf
Short Description: Homeless kobolds raid Castle Raisinloaf to seize Vampire Lord C'hahd's hidden Heart. Win a new home. Fail and become dinner. Chaos, guns, and magipunk mayhem!
Long Description:
In Kobold Quest: Dust & Devils, a narrative-driven, wild west, steampunk magipunk adventure bursting with chaos, light rules, and ridiculous fun, your homeless, hungry kobold clan is one bad torch away from extinction—and desperate times call for reckless real estate acquisition. The plan? Seize Castle Raisinloaf and make it yours.
 
 Unfortunately, it’s occupied by the brooding (and extremely bitey) Vampire Lord C'hahd and a buffet of monsters, mercenaries, and things that skitter in the dark. Rumor whispers his still-beating Heart lies hidden deep within the castle’s cursed halls. Capture it, and the pale tyrant must kneel. Fail, and you’re appetizers. Grab your six-shooters, your posse, and your questionable courage—tonight, the kobolds raid.

Title: The Gen Con Comedy Gala
Short Description:
A hilarious night of nerdy stand up comedy featuring the funniest stand up comedians in all of table top kicks off at Gen Con. 
 Hosted by Instagram's D&D stand Up legend Steve Wrigley.
Long Description:
It's comedy about our culture. Stand up sets by nerds for nerds for one night only at Gen Con 2026!
 
 Geek culture has moved from the table top to both the stage and screen and over the last decade, nerd culture has dominated the entertainment space. 
 
 Steve Wrigley is mustering the best Stand Up Comics from all across our vibrant eco system of gamers, designers, content creators and cos players to assemble the dork fueled avengers of comedy. 
 
 With a line up of legends to be announced in the coming weeks, you do not want to miss this chance to see your favorites be funny in a rollercoaster show of laughs all about tabletop, gaming and our culture as a whole. 
 
 The perfect way to cap off a day on the convention floor!

Title: TTRPG From Cradle to Grave Publishing Workshop
Short Description: If you're certain you want to publish your TTRPG but not how to go about it the best way, this workshop is for you as industry professionals guide you hand in hand in this intensive class.
Long Description:
Within this workshop you will gain the tools, knowledge, advice, and skills necessary to take your TTRPG from just a kitchen table dream of being published to a fully realized property. Several published industry professionals will guide you step by step on the process to getting your property published.
 
 Topics that will be gone over in depth are:
 
 - Walk through all manner from creation, to editing, to review, to legal
 - Advice on how to launch a kickstarter and collaborate with teams and creators
 - Logistical challenges and factors to consider
 - How to manage burnout in creation
 - And most importantly what's next after you've finally published
 
 This will be a small class with three to four proctors who have all the experience to share in a group setting and in smaller pairs.

Title: Adventures in Middle Earth: The Eaves of Mirkwood
Short Description: Following the defeat of the Dragon, King Bard has sent out a clarion call to the resilient folk of Lake-town, inviting brave souls to help reclaim and rebuild the shattered North.
Long Description: Set on the treacherous northern fringes of the Great Forest, The Eaves of Mirkwood serves as a harrowing introduction to the perils of Middle-earth for a company of new heroes. Tasked with escorting a group of woodmen or seeking passage through the suffocating gloom, the adventurers soon find themselves ensnared in a web of ancient malice as they encounter the greedy forest-dwellers and the creeping shadow of the Necromancer’s influence. The journey culminates in a desperate struggle for survival against a formidable foe in the heart of the woods, forcing the party to balance their survival instincts against the noble call of the Free Peoples. It is a quintessential tale of "there and back again" that highlights the fragile boundary between the warmth of the campfire and the hungry dark of the Wild.

Title: STYX Launch Party
Short Description: A special event celebrating the release of our newest game! Participants will explore the history with special guests from Don't Panic Games and play.
Long Description: Don't Panic Games & Double Exposure invite you to celebrate the release of this new bluffing game! Each player takes on the role of an overlord vying for the Throne of the Underworld. A game of STYX is divided into turns called Audiences. During each Audience, 2 Emissaries from the Tribes of the Underworld are sent forth to pledge their allegiance to the Throne. Each player secretly chooses a token determining their chosen course of action toward the Emissaries. Depending on the players’ choices, they may gain the allegiance of the tribe, influence, gold, and the Elements they need to win the race to the Throne. They may also choose to betray the Emissaries, slaying them in order to harvest their souls and slow their competition down.

Title: The Ledgers of Whitechapel
Short Description: Edwin Harrowick has vanished after claiming the Ripper files were “not finished.” His parents, through a discreet solicitor, hire you to find him—before politics creates unwanted attention.
Long Description: London, five years after the Whitechapel murders. The killings are officially over. Edwin Harrowick, author and independent researcher, has vanished from polite society. His lodgings are abandoned. His correspondence has ceased. Before his disappearance, Edwin claimed the Ripper cases were “not properly concluded.” Not the crimes themselves—but the records. Police files reappear after being archived. Census entries shift. Civil claims reference individuals who cannot be located. Death certificates exist for men still drawing wages.Such matters are sensitive. The Whitechapel murders remain a public wound and a political liability. Reopening them—even inadvertently—would not be welcomed. You are not being asked to pursue a killer, of course, you are being asked to locate Edwin Harrowick—and to determine what compelled him to withdraw from public view.
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Thieves of Eldris Launch Party
Short Description: A special event celebrating the release of our newest game! Participants will explore the history with special guests from the publisher and play.
Long Description: Inside Up Games & Double Exposure invite you to celebrate the release of this new hidden movement dice game! Fortunes are forged in the shadows, one stolen coin at a time. Amid the chaos of bustling merchants, you lurk in the shadows, eyes locked on your target, ready to slice their coin purse and make off with the spoils. The Thieves' Guild has taken notice of your talents, but earning your place among them won't be easy. To prove your skill, you must outwit your rivals, steal from your targets, and keep your true identity hidden. Slip past rival thieves undetected, and when the time is right, liberate your targets of their precious coin. But beware, one wrong move and your rivals might expose you, stealing not only your newfound riches, but your invitation to the Thieves' Guild. Will you become the guild's newest initiate, or will your ambitions leave you penniless in the gutter?
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Hell of a Deal Launch Party
Short Description: A special event celebrating the release of our newest game! Participants will explore the history with special guest Curt Covert and play.
Long Description: Smirk & Dagger Games & Double Exposure invite you to celebrate the release of this new cooperative game! Play poker against The Devil Himself & all the minions of Hell. Of course, they all cheat! To compete, you’ll have to cheat like Hell... and the more you do, the more doomed you all become. It’s real poker - as far as playing poker in Hell goes, with you all trying individually to beat the house. Every Boss changes how you will approach the hand and has a bare minimum hand to beat, should their dealt cards not pan out. It's a Texas Hold'em style game - and players get not two but three cards dealt to them (one up their sleeve for more options). You may earn powerful Favor cards that let you cheat, like making a four-card Flush, but at the cost of a Curse or suffering Temptation.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Ghostbusters TTRPG For Charity!
Short Description: Get hired as an intern for the Circle City Ghostbusters during Gen Con and help clean the convention center of its latest haunting -- fees from this game go to Extra Life Indy Guild!
Long Description:
Do you believe in UFOs, astral projections, mental telepathy, ESP, clairvoyance, spirit photography, telekinetic movement, full trance mediums, the Loch Ness monster and the theory of Atlantis? If the answer is yes, come play in this game with all fees going to charity!
 You'll be entering the Bobverse to help Indianapolis's local Ghostbusters team, the Circle City Ghostbusters, investigate and eliminate paranormal activity at Gen Con. Best of all? The money earned by the hosts of this session is going straight to Extra Life Indy Guild! The Bobverse is an ongoing series of Ghostbusters RPG sessions live streamed by acclaimed paranormal podcast Bob After Dark. Circle City Ghostbusters is a 501(c)(3) charity costuming group of Ghostbusters enthusiasts based in Indianapolis, Indiana.
 This game is run by Bob Anderson, host of Bob After Dark!
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Materials Required Details: Please create/submit your character sheet before the game begins!
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Beginner Swing Dance: Lindy Hop 101
Short Description:
Let's swing dance! Learn some of the essential partnered steps, moves, and rhythms of the swing era, and have fun doing it!
 
 No partner necessary to join this class!
Long Description:
Lindy Hop is a dance originating from Harlem, NY during the 1920s & 30s alongside Jazz music. It is a partnered dance created by Black Americans that spread throughout the country and the world. The dance is fun, lively, and social.
 
 Naptown Stomp Lindy Hop Society is Indy’s longest running organization devoted to teaching and spreading enthusiasm for vintage swing dances – Lindy Hop being the primary form.
 
 In this class, you will learn the 6-count basic step, simple turns, and more!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Ethan Netsch, Ethan Netsch, Cheyenne Olson, Amy Christiansen
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Puppet Costume Contest
Short Description: Come one, come all to the first-ever Gen Con Puppet Costume Contest! Come see puppets wearing their favorite cosplay! Enter your own puppet into the mix and find out who will take home the prizes!
Long Description: Enter silly contests & win silly prizes! Grab your puppet, slap a costume on it (with care, integrity, and attention to detail), and be prepared to get judged by both celebrity and non-celebrity judges judging your every move. The judges' judginess is gonna judge up your judgee somethin' fierce! So fiercely it could win you a prize! Or just come to cheer on the silliness and see what these craftspeople have come up with. Is your alligator puppet dressing up as a carrot? Is your rabbit cosplaying as Jessica Rabbit (who is not actually a rabbit)? Are your finger puppets dressing up like actual fingers!?!?!!? Whatever it is, we want to see your puppets in their finest costumes! The entry form is emailed to everyone who gets a ticket, just in case you need to make a very wise last-minute decision to enter.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Emily Shentu, Jennifer Barnhart, Stacey Gordon, Brandon Smith
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Control Your Puppet's Hands
Short Description: Everyone's obsessed with those little sticks on puppet hands & wants to know if there's a trick to use them. We're here to reveal those tricks and send you on your way with some brand new skills!
Long Description: Excuse me, madam! Control your puppet's hands!....is what you won't hear after this workshop! Bring a puppet with arm rods if you've got one, and if you don't, you can wait in line to see what you can borrow from us. Sesame Street performer and avid arm-rod utilist, Brandon Smith, is going to be teaching you some wonderful, new skills!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Make a Rockin' Robin Puppet
Short Description: Join puppet-building wunderkind Richard Gomez and make a moving-mouth Rockin' Robin puppet! This one is definitely worth waking up early for! See our longer description for a list of required skills.
Long Description: Hello... imagine meeting you here, in the longer description. I'm here to tell you about all of the skills every single participant is going to need to already have in order to take this workshop: sewing (running stitch), using a hot glue gun without dying or crying, following 2 or 3-step instructions, measuring, listening, self-advocacy, boundaries with your mom, silliness, a sense of wonder, and using scissors.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Puppet DnD Very Much Not In a Castle
Short Description: Join our guest DM, Mark Meer (some video game/castle guy), and enjoy watching or playing in this improvised quest with gamers and their puppets! 2 audience participants, plus 2 lawful good puppeteers!
Long Description:
Mark Meer (known best for the 2025 Gen Con Puppet Slam & being Canadian) will be your dungeon master in this epic quest for winning and probably something else. (Dude, I really need to take another DnD class.) Pick your race and pick your class & pick your spells & chemtrails...(?) and bring a puppet to cheer on the players, or don't bring a puppet and just watch alone without having a puppet with you (weirdo). 2 (TWO!) lucky audience members will get to play alongside our very seasoned puppeteers, one of whom is also very seasoned at DnD, and one of whom is....very not-seasoned. Find out how much Lowry's seasoning we're gonna need in this legendary adventure that unfolds before your very eyes! THE SHENANIGANS WILL BE SHENANIGAINING! 
 CAN TRIPS! They're called cantrips. Because you take a trip in a can in order to cast a special spell. (I remembered! And then made etymology!)
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
GM Names: Mark Meer, Stacey Gordon, Yinan Shentu, Jennifer Barnhart
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Snowblind
Short Description: When a remote Antarctic research station falls silent after detecting anomalous radio signals, Miskatonic University dispatches your expedition to investigate.
Long Description: When a remote Antarctic research station falls silent after detecting anomalous radio signals, Miskatonic University dispatches your expedition to investigate. Can you brave the storm, uncover the truth, and survive whatever is stirring beneath the ice? Two-fisted pulp horror at the bottom of the world.

Title: Of Burdens and Fellowship
Short Description: You are small people who walked into a big war. Your fellowship played a humble, crucial role in the fall of the Doomslord. Now, laden with stories to tell and burdens to bear, you set off for home.
Long Description: The game begins after the great war with the Doomslord, and the the stories you tell with determine the details of the adventures you've had and the home to which you return. The game is inspired by the works of J.R. R. Tolkien, especially the melancholy and emotional passages of The Lord of the Rings.
Game System: Back Again from the Broken Land

Title: Miniature Book Nook
Short Description: Create your own book nook! We’ll provide 3D printed or unassembled blank nooks, encouragement, assembly supplies & instructions, + a smorgasbord of things to create your own unique Book Nook.
Long Description: Choose from a laser cut blank nook-meaning unassembled-or a 3D printed book nook to decorate. We provide everything you need for either nook type-paint, glue, all the assembly necessities to put together the blank Nook. And then the fun really starts as we will have an abundance of embellishments for you to use to create your personalized Book Nook. Seasonal, horror, nerdy and just pretty, we'll have it. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced crafter, our workshop is meant to be fun & engaging. Plus, we’ll be there to guide and encourage you every step of the way. At the end of the class, you’ll leave with a beautifully crafted book nook ready to display!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: Wool Felting
Short Description: What is more cathartic than stabbing something a thousand times without killing it? Needle felting and creating something unique and beautiful with wool. You'll take home your own unique creation.
Long Description: Needle felting is an ancient art that turns fluffy wool into soft sculptures. Specialized barbed needles are used to stab wool roving into place as you create and build wool on wool. Basic techniques and safety tips will be taught at the beginning of class. All materials will be provided, including a ball of core wool, roving in a variety of colors, and 3 felting needles. What will your humble wool become? A penguin, a Pokeball, a snowman, a monster? It's up to you!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: The Silver Swallow Club
Short Description: Step back in time to a swanky 1920s speakeasy where the revelry is disrupted by a vicious murder in this immersive, app-guided role playing experience.
Long Description:
By day, The Silver Swallow Club is a place for bird watchers and nature enthusiasts. After dark, it offers something else entirely: A rowdy speakeasy where free thinkers flout Prohibition laws to enjoy jazz, bootleg liquor, and roaring good times. Tonight, however, someone at the Silver Swallow Club has murder on their mind… 
 
 Get ready to solve a Gatsby-era murder mystery through three rounds of social gameplay with help from the StoryPop app. You’ll role play an assigned character with motivations and unique objectives to complete. Your actions and decisions will shape how the story unfolds, so you’ll need cunning, courage, and charm in order to come out on top. Interact with fellow players to discover hidden agendas, unearth secrets, and identify the killer before it’s too late! The StoryPop team is thrilled to offer this one-of-a-kind experience at Gen Con – don’t miss out!
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Materials Required Details: Charged smartphone
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Become a Mini Painting Maestro by Mastering the Fundamentals
Short Description: Have you ever felt that your painting has hit a plateau and you just can’t push to that next level? The culprit is most likely the criminally undertaught skills of paint consistency and brush control.
Long Description: What is the single most important skill in Mini-Painting; Color Theory? OSL? NMM? Buttery Smooth Blending? All those sound great, but are impossible to pull off without knowing how to use your paints and brushes to their fullest potential. In this class we will take a deep dive into the different levels of paint dilution, how they behave differently from each other, and when to use them. In addition, we will talk about how to load (and unload) your brush for maximum precision. From base coating to glazing to freehand, the skills you will learn in this class will take your painting to new heights. While this course is mainly aimed at beginner and intermediate painters, advanced painters are welcome too if they want an in-depth refresher.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Materials Required Details: Brushes and palettes, paints (optional)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Hate Edge Highlighting? Learn to Paint Volumes Instead
Short Description: Tired of painting like the GW box art and highlighting every single edge 3 times? Learn the fundamentals of volumetric highlighting and bring some realism to your paint jobs (while having MORE fun!)
Long Description: The 'eavy metal painting style has become dominant in the wargaming painting scene since it appears on all the box art of the most popular miniatures range out there. And while it looks great for product photography, man is it TEDIOUS, and frankly a little cartoony looking. I have always found that painting for a little bit more of a realistic look, and borrowing from the techniques of our 2D artist peers is much more exciting. In this class we will take a step by step approach to learning volumetric highlighting covering topics such as: color choices, light placement, paint application, contrast ratios, and more.
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Materials Required Details: Brushes and palettes, paints (optional)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Learn to Paint Aged Leather (& Other Complex Materials) Through the Power of Thinking in Layers
Short Description: In this class we’ll practice painting worn leather and discuss how conceptualizing the painting process in layers can unlock a whole new level of complexity in your mini-painting.
Long Description: What do ogres and mini-painting have in common? They have layers! Most people are familiar with layering in mini-painting as a technique for quickly creating color transitions and building highlights. But if we take a closer look at why layering works and extrapolate that out to the rest of the painting process, we'll discover a world of new possibilities with our painting. If you've ever wondered how the pros can create such evocative colors/textures/lighting/depth this is the class for you! For Intermediate painters. It is highly recommended to take my "Become a Mini-painting Maestro by Mastering the Fundamentals." class before taking this one if you aren't already very comfortable with your paint application.
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Materials Required Details: Brushes and palettes, paints (optional)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: How Physics Can Make You a Better Mini Painter
Short Description: Where art and science intersect! Light interacts with different materials in VERY different ways. By understanding this you'll be able to recreate a multitude of surface types on your minis.
Long Description: In this class we will talk about the science of light and how it interacts with different shapes and different materials. We will learn the difference between specular and diffuse reflections, as well as discuss why metallic surfaces are so uniquely difficult to understand and represent in art. This course will provide a solid theoretical and practical foundation for determining how to approach painting different materials on your minis. For Intermediate to advanced painters. It is highly recommended to take my "Become a Mini-painting Maestro by Mastering the Fundamentals." class before taking this one if you aren't already very confident with your paint application.
Experience Required: Some (You've played it a bit and understand the basics)
Materials Required Details: Brushes and palettes, paints (optional)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: What Makes a Color?
Short Description: In this class we will discuss color choice and how it affects a viewer's perception. You'll work collaboratively with a partner to tell different stories on the same model through your color choices.
Long Description: If I paint an object purple, green, and yellow what color is it? The answer depends on how much of each and in which order. Perhaps more importantly those colors will tell you something about that object such the environment its in, the lighting conditions, or even essential qualities of the material itself. This class is for advanced painters who are already very confident in their brush work and understand the concept of volumetric highlighting. (If you need training on either of those topics I have classes earlier in the convention for both!)
Experience Required: Expert (You play it regularly and know all the rules)
Materials Required Details: Brushes and palettes, paints (optional)

Title: Lose the Fear of Green Stuff
Short Description: Learn miniature sculpting with 14-time Golden Demon winner Joe Orteza! This hands-on class teaches green stuff techniques to customize minis. Includes materials and a big take-home sculpting.
Long Description:
This sculpting class will be taught by Joe Orteza, a 14-time Golden Demon winner and professional art teacher. Designed for beginner to intermediate hobbyists, this hands-on workshop participants will learn practical green stuff sculpting techniques that will add character, depth, and detail to their models!
 During the class, Joe will demonstrate several of his personal sculpting methods, including techniques developed specifically for this workshop. Students will learn how to sculpt elements such as capes, banners, and relief details that bring more movement and individuality to their miniatures.
 All materials will be provided. Each attendee will receive green stuff to use during the session and will also take home a big sculpting kit included with the course fee, allowing them to continue practicing their new skills after the class!

Title: Book-Binding for Beginners: Make a Coptic Stitch Journal
Short Description: Learn the fundamentals of bookbinding while crafting a handmade journal for your TTRPG character—built slowly, thoughtfully, and entirely by you!
Long Description:
In this guided workshop, participants will learn the fundamentals of bookbinding while creating an open-stitch journal from start to finish. Step by step, we’ll assemble pages, stitch the binding, and transform simple materials into a sturdy, functional notebook. 
 
 Participants will be able to customize their journals using a curated selection of design elements. No two journals will be exactly alike—each book reflecting the personality of its maker.
 
 Experience Level: 
 This is a beginner-friendly crafting experience designed for those who enjoy working with their hands. No prior bookbinding experience is required!
 
 What’s Provided
 	•	Bookbinding tools you can take home with you!
 	•	All crafting materials
 
 Accessibility Notes: Requires fine motor skills and extended hand use. Seating provided throughout the event.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: The Lament of the Valley
Short Description: "Hurry! Cover your ears! Don’t answer the song!" Too late. It's always too late. It will haunt every minute, hour, day, and thought. And when your breath falters, it will use you to finish the melody.
Long Description: Blood on the Lens is a Horror Movie RPG system. The Director will lead you — the victims — through a twisted, nightmarish tale where every choice, every step, and every whispered sound could bend your mind. The song lingers in the air, in the shadows, in your own thoughts. Sleep fades. Reality distorts. Can you survive long enough to remember who you were before the melody claimed you… or will you become part of its unending lament?
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Decoding Downtown Indy Walking Tour
Short Description: Explore historic downtown Indianapolis and discover stories hidden within the city’s landmarks. Along the way, learn about architectural styles and local history.
Long Description:
Tourgoers will discover the distinctive heart of Indianapolis, including exteriors of the Indiana Statehouse, Soldiers & Sailors Monument, St. Elmo’s Steakhouse, and more. 
 
 Participants will leave this gamified walking tour with the skills necessary to begin decoding buildings in cities across the country, analyzing their styles and details for a better understanding of local history
 
 This tour takes place outside and covers approximately 1 mile. Tours run rain or shine, so wear comfortable shoes and dress for the weather. 
 
 The route is accessible for those using mobility aids or with strollers. There is not seating or access to public restrooms along the tour route.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)

Title: Monument Circle Historic District Walking Tour
Short Description: Step out into the heart of Indianapolis, the “Circle City,” on a guided walking tour.
Long Description:
Discover why Indianapolis has come to be known as the “Circle City” on a guided walking tour of the city’s most recognizable historic district. Tourgoers will see landmarks from various eras, including exteriors of the Soldiers & Sailors Monument, Christ Church Cathedral, Circle Theatre, and more. 
 
 Along the way, guides will share stories and little-known history, revealing how the district’s art, architecture, and unique combination of public and private spaces give Indy its distinctive character as the state’s capital.
 
 This tour takes place outside and covers approximately 1 mile. Tours run rain or shine, so wear comfortable shoes and dress for the weather. 
 
 The route is accessible for those using mobility aids or with strollers. There is not seating or access to public restrooms along the tour route.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: White River State Park Walking Tour
Short Description: Immerse yourself in the cultural and historical significance of White River State Park.
Long Description:
Guides will share stories of the park’s history, from the time of Indianapolis’s settlement to the present day, tracing how the area evolved from a hub of transportation and industry to a center for recreation, culture, and entertainment. The tour will highlight how historic landmarks became assets within the park including the 1916 Washington Street bridge, limestone-lined river promenade, 1870 pumphouse, Central Canal, and more. 
 
 This tour takes place outside and covers approximately 1 mile. Tours run rain or shine, so wear comfortable shoes and dress for the weather. 
 
 The route is accessible for those using mobility aids or with strollers. There is no seating along the tour route. Public restrooms are available in the park.
Experience Required: None (You've never played before - rules will be taught)
Attendee Registration?: Yes, they can register for this round without having played in any other events

Title: Walking Tour by the Dice
Short Description: Join a party led by skilled guides on an individualized walking trek through historic downtown Indianapolis.
Long Description:
Over the course of two hours, group members will roll dice at strategic locations to help guides shape everything from the tour route to which buildings and stories they share. 
 
 Each group will have a uniquely tailored experience, shaped by the luck of the dice. All groups will leave having seen and learned about the art, history, architecture, and urban planning of downtown Indianapolis.

Title: The Art of Daggerheart

Long Description:
Illustrations, how they are made and showcased on the page, are the brushstrokes by which a development team brings game worlds to life.  Join the creative team as they sit down to explore the methods by which narrative gameplay and collaborative worldbuilding ideals were woven into the illustration pipeline process. Title: Women Shaping the Future of TCGs
Short Description: Join leaders behind iconic trading card games for a deep dive into the TCG renaissance, exploring billion-dollar brand strategy, "drop" culture, scarcity and building inclusive global communities.
Long Description:
Join the leaders behind the industry’s most iconic trading card games for an insightful look at the strategy driving the modern TCG renaissance. This panel moves beyond surface-level trends to explore the complexities of managing billion-dollar brands, navigating "drop" culture and scarcity, and building inclusive global communities. From the meteoric rise of Disney Lorcana to the enduring legacy of Pokémon and the creative disruption of Secret Lair, these executives will dig in to the evolving landscape of TCG.
 
 Moderator: Deirdre Cross (Game Industry Executive, Board Member Women in Toys, Licensing & Entertainment)
 Panelists: Zoe McNamara, UVS Games (Riot Games: Riftbound TCG)
 Merritt Rodriguez, Product Line Manager, TCGs for Asmodee studio Fantasy Flight Games (Star Wars: Unlimited TCG)

Title: Zenithal & Basic Miniature Painting
Short Description: This class is designed for basic to intermediate hobbyists who want to improve their painting skills and better understand the techniques used to create striking tabletop miniatures.
Long Description:
Learn from 14 time golden demon winner Joe Orteza! Each student will receive a zenithal-primed miniature to paint during the class and take home afterward. Paints and brushes will be provided for use during the session, along with a painting guide that summarizes the techniques discussed so students can continue practicing on their own.
 
 During the workshop, Joe will demonstrate several of his basic to intermediate painting methods and explain the differences between tabletop-quality miniatures and competition-level pieces. Students will learn how zenithal priming can help guide lighting and shading while painting.
